beijing.recommended_base_reason: Dongcheng gives walkable access to Forbidden City, Hutongs and Drum Tower

kids_attractions.name: Universal Studios Beijing
kids_attractions.tip: World's largest Universal Studios — Harry Potter, Transformers, Minions, Kung Fu Panda, Jurassic World. Buy Express Pass for popular rides. Harry Potter's Forbidden Journey and the Kung Fu Panda ride are the crowd favourites. Avoid Chinese public holidays (May Day, National Day, Spring Festival) — queues become extreme. Book via Trip.com for discounts. Kids under 1.2m get discounted entry.

kids_attractions.name: Beijing Zoo + Aquarium
kids_attractions.chinese: 北京动物园 & 海洋馆
kids_attractions.tip: Beijing's oldest zoo with 5,000+ animals including giant pandas (star attraction). The attached Aquarium (¥120 separate ticket) has an 80m underwater tunnel, beluga whale viewing, dolphin shows and mermaid performances. Do the aquarium first (less walking) then the pandas. Kids under 1.2m enter both free.

kids_attractions.name: China Science & Technology Museum
kids_attractions.tip: 300+ interactive exhibits across science, space, robotics and technology. The giant IMAX dome theatre shows space and nature films (¥20 extra). Children can control robots, fly a virtual aircraft and explore the history of Chinese inventions. Best for ages 6–14. Book ahead during school holidays.

kids_attractions.name: Beijing Natural History Museum
kids_attractions.tip: Outstanding dinosaur collection — complete T-Rex and Brachiosaurus skeletons. The Hall of Ancient Life traces evolution with remarkable fossils. The Human Body Science exhibit is interactive and educational. Free but must book via WeChat. Hugely popular with school groups — avoid weekday mornings during term time.

kids_attractions.name: Watercube Indoor Waterpark
kids_attractions.tip: Inside the iconic 2008 Olympics Water Cube — China's first fully themed indoor waterpark. 13 water slides, wave pool, lazy river and a separate toddler splash zone. The bubble-architecture exterior is the most photographed building in Beijing. Book tickets online. Open year-round regardless of weather.

kids_attractions.name: Happy Valley Beijing
kids_attractions.tip: Chinese theme park with 100+ rides across 7 themed zones. Better for thrillseeker families with older kids (8+) than Universal. The Wooden Roller Coaster is one of China's most beloved. Much cheaper than Universal. Avoid peak holidays. Annual passes offer excellent value for Beijing residents.

hangzhou.name: Hangzhou
hangzhou.tagline: Heaven on Earth — China's Most Romantic City

attractions.name: West Lake (Xi Hu)
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Walk the Su Causeway (苏堤) for cross-lake views — 2km from end to end. Rent a bicycle (¥5/hr) to circle the full lake (15km). Early morning mist is magical. The ¥70 rowboat ferry is the best way to see 三潭印月 (Three Pools).
attractions.description: China's most celebrated body of water — the 10 classical views of West Lake that Chinese painters have been reproducing for 1,000 years are all real: mist on the water at dawn, lotus flowers in summer, snow-covered pagodas in winter. Su Dongpo called it the most beautiful place on earth.

attractions.name: Lingyin Temple + Feilai Peak
attractions.tip: Founded 1,700 years ago — one of China's most important Buddhist temples. Go before 8am to see monks chanting morning prayers. The giant laughing Buddha at Feilai Peak is the most famous. The 470 stone Buddha carvings along the stream path span 1,000 years of art.
attractions.description: One of China's ten greatest Buddhist temples — founded in 328 AD in a valley of ancient camphor trees, its main hall houses a 24-metre gilded camphor wood Buddha. The carved grottoes of the adjacent Feilai Peak contain 345 sculptures dating from the 10th-14th centuries.

attractions.name: Dragon Well Tea Village
attractions.tip: China's most famous tea village surrounded by terraced gardens. Sit with a local farmer and drink Dragon Well tea while watching the mountains. Buy directly from farmers (¥80–300/100g) not from tourist shops. Spring first harvest (明前, before April 5th) is the most prized.
attractions.description: The source of China's most celebrated green tea — Longjing (Dragon Well) tea is grown on hillside terraces surrounding a village that has been producing the same variety since the Tang dynasty. Visit in April during the Qingming harvest.
attractions.photo_query: longjing tea dragon well hangzhou village

attractions.name: Broken Bridge
attractions.tip: Most romantic bridge in China from the White Snake legend — despite the name, it's not broken. Best in snow (rare in Hangzhou), or in spring when weeping willows line the walkway. The bridge connects White Causeway to the main shore.
attractions.description: The most famous bridge on West Lake — a Han dynasty arched stone bridge that appears 'broken' in winter when snow melts faster at the heated bridge joints, creating a gap in the white surface. The setting for one of China's most beloved folk tales about a white snake spirit.
attractions.photo_query: broken bridge west lake hangzhou snow

attractions.name: Hefang Street
attractions.tip: Ming dynasty commercial street. Home to Hu Qingyu Tang pharmacy (胡庆余堂, 1874) — the most beautiful traditional Chinese medicine pharmacy in China, free to enter. Try the street snacks: Ding Sheng Gao (定胜糕), Cong Bao Gui (葱包桧) and cold chicken. Lanterns at night are gorgeous.
attractions.description: Hangzhou's most authentic historical commercial street — the Song dynasty Qinghefang area where traditional medicine shops and craft workshops occupy buildings from the Republic era. The osmanthus wine and West Lake fish in vinegar sauce available here are the definitive Hangzhou food experiences.

attractions.name: Leifeng Pagoda
attractions.tip: Take the elevator to the golden top for the best view of the whole West Lake and mountains. The glass floors inside reveal the Song dynasty original brick pagoda ruins below your feet. The best sunset view in Hangzhou — stay for golden hour over the lake.
attractions.description: A Song dynasty pagoda rebuilt on its original site — standing on the southern shore of West Lake, its amber form reflected perfectly in the still water is the most reproduced image of Hangzhou. Climb to the top for the best view of the lake's full extent at sunset.

attractions.name: Xixi National Wetland Park
attractions.tip: China's only urban national wetland park — 11km² of waterways and lotus ponds. Take the included boat tour first (45 mins), then explore the walking trails. Best in late spring (lotus flowers) or autumn (reeds turn gold). The hot air balloon ride over the wetlands (¥300) is unforgettable.
attractions.description: China's first and only urban wetland national park — 11.5km² of channels, lakes and islands covered in plum, osmanthus and tangerine orchards that have been farmed by fishermen for 2,000 years. The dragon boat festival celebration here is the most atmospheric in China.
attractions.photo_query: xixi wetland park hangzhou water nature

attractions.name: Wuzhen Water Town (Day Trip)
attractions.tip: One of the best-preserved ancient water towns in China — 1.5hrs from Hangzhou by bus. The West Scenic Zone (西栅) has the most atmospheric evening views. Ancient Ming/Qing architecture, stone bridges and gondola rides. Book ahead in Golden Week — sell out weeks in advance.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Hangzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Baochu Pagoda & Precious Stone Hill
attractions.tip: Free hike with panoramic views of the whole West Lake — far less visited than other viewpoints. The 1,000-year-old slender pagoda at the top. Best at sunrise (arrive by 5:45am in summer). The hillside path is lined with pomegranate trees that flower red in summer.
attractions.description: A slender Song dynasty pagoda on a rocky ridge overlooking West Lake — its elegant silhouette against the misty lake and surrounding hills has been the defining element of Hangzhou's skyline for 1,000 years. The hill walk up to the pagoda through bamboo forest is as beautiful as the destination.
attractions.photo_query: baochu pagoda hangzhou west lake hill

attractions.name: Longjing Tea Plantation Trail
attractions.tip: A free walking trail through the actual Dragon Well tea gardens — rows of tea bushes as far as you can see. Best in spring when pickers are harvesting and the air smells of fresh green tea. Start from the top of the village and walk down. Completely free.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Hangzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zhongshan Road Night Market
attractions.tip: The 1,400m Southern Song Imperial Street by night — Hangzhou's most atmospheric night market. Traditional snack stalls, heritage Chinese medicine shops and lanterns overhead. Best after 6pm. The Red Carp Pool (金鱼池) at the end has street performers most evenings.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Hangzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yue Fei Temple
attractions.tip: Memorial temple for Song dynasty general Yue Fei — China's most revered patriot. The iron statues of the four traitors who caused his death are kneeling in front of his tomb, eternally shamed. Moving and historically significant. Combine with a walk along the northern lakeshore.
attractions.description: The memorial temple to China's most beloved military hero — General Yue Fei, wrongly executed in 1142AD, is commemorated with a magnificent Song dynasty temple complex on the northern bank of West Lake. The iron statues of his betrayers kneeling in shame at the tomb entrance are particularly powerful.
attractions.photo_query: yue fei temple hangzhou west lake

attractions.name: Faxitang Flower Market
attractions.tip: The 'prayer temple' that's gone viral for its beautiful yellow walls and flower offerings. Dramatically less crowded than Lingyin Temple. Locals believe it's especially efficacious for romantic relationships — full of couples lighting incense. Combine with nearby Dragon Well Village.
attractions.description: A must-visit temple in Hangzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Hangzhou Museum & Qinghefang Area
attractions.tip: Excellent free museum with the famous 战国水晶杯 (Warring States crystal cup, 2,500 years old) and Liangzhu jade pieces. Must book in advance via WeChat. The surrounding Qinghefang historic area has excellent street food nearby. Closed Mondays.
attractions.description: The commercial and cultural heart of Hangzhou's historic district — the Qinghefang pedestrian area combines traditional medicine shops, craft workshops and street food vendors in a beautifully restored Song-dynasty streetscape adjacent to West Lake.

attractions.name: Qiandao Lake (Thousand Island Lake)
attractions.tip: 1,078 islands in an enormous freshwater reservoir — one of China's most photographed landscapes. About 2hrs from Hangzhou by bus. The boat tours between islands are the main experience. The water is crystal clear — swimming in summer is allowed in designated areas.
attractions.description: An artificial lake created in 1959 containing 1,078 forested islands — the water is so clean it is piped to Shanghai as drinking water. The island-hopping boat tours and the dramatic vista of forested hills emerging from turquoise water are extraordinary.

food.name: West Lake Vinegar Fish
food.where: Lou Wai Lou (楼外楼) — lakeside since 1848
food.tip: Hangzhou's most iconic dish — whole grass carp in sweet vinegar glaze. Must be cooked in under 4 minutes to preserve delicate texture. Lou Wai Lou has the most scenic setting overlooking Gu Shan island. Book lakeside tables ahead.

food.name: Dragon Well Shrimp
food.where: Zhiwei Guan (知味观) — Hangzhou's oldest restaurant group
food.tip: River shrimp stir-fried with fresh Dragon Well tea leaves — a uniquely Hangzhou dish. The tea gives it a subtle grassy fragrance. Only authentic in spring (April) with fresh first-harvest leaves. The rest of the year uses dried leaves which are less aromatic.

food.name: Dongpo Pork
food.where: Tianxiang Lou (天香楼) or Zhiwei Guan (知味观)
food.tip: Braised pork belly named after poet Su Dongpo who invented it here in the 11th century. Melt-in-your-mouth layered belly slow-braised in Shaoxing wine and soy. The fat becomes perfectly jiggly — not greasy at all. Served in individual clay pots.

food.name: Ding Sheng Gao (Victory Cake)
food.where: Hefang Street stalls and traditional pastry shops
food.tip: Pink cloud-shaped steamed rice cakes with sweet red bean filling — a Song dynasty snack that generals gave soldiers before battle for good luck (定胜 = 'certain victory'). Soft, lightly sweet, completely moreish. Hangzhou's most photogenic street snack.

food.name: Xia Bao Shan Mian (Shrimp Noodles)
food.where: Kuiyuan Guan (奎元馆) — Hangzhou's oldest noodle restaurant, est. 1867
food.tip: Hangzhou's signature noodle — river shrimp and crispy fried eel on thick noodles in dark soy broth. The eel is flash-fried until golden and stays crispy even in soup. Kuiyuan Guan has been serving this since 1867. Very satisfying combination of textures.

food.name: Longjing Tea & Snacks
food.where: Tea houses in Dragon Well Village or West Lake waterfront
food.tip: China's most prized green tea. Sit in a local farmhouse tea room, watch the hills and sip. First harvest (明前龙井 before April 5th) is most prized. The tea has a chestnut aroma completely different from any Longjing you've had outside China.

food.name: Cong Bao Gui (Green Onion Bake)
food.where: Hefang Street stalls near Qinghefang
food.tip: Hangzhou's unique street food — a crispy green onion and plain fried dough stick pressed flat on a griddle and glazed with sweet and spicy sauce. Named after Yue Fei's traitor Qin Gui — pressing the dough stick is symbolic revenge. ¥6 and utterly satisfying.

food.name: West Lake Su Causeway Cafes
food.must_order: Longjing green tea latte, lotus seed cake
food.tip: Several cafes along the Su Causeway with direct West Lake views. Morning mist over the lake in spring and autumn is magical — some of China's most photographed scenery. The combination of weeping willows, peach blossoms (March–April) and a West Lake view from your table is exceptional.

food.name: Longjing Village Tea Farms
food.near: Longjing Village, 20 min from West Lake by bus
food.must_order: Freshly picked and roasted Longjing green tea, tea eggs
food.tip: Visit the actual tea farms where China's most famous green tea is grown. Farmers sell direct — 3x cheaper than city shops and infinitely fresher. Watch the tea being pan-roasted right in front of you. The terraced tea fields with misty mountains behind are stunning in spring (April is harvest season). Sit at a farm table and taste 5 different grades.

food.name: Hefang Street (河坊街) Snacks
food.near: Hefang Street, Shangcheng District, 15 min from West Lake
food.must_order: West Lake vinegar fish (醋鱼), dongpo pork, osmanthus glutinous rice
food.tip: Hangzhou's historic food street — osmanthus wine, West Lake lotus root, fried spring rolls. The West Lake vinegar fish (醋鱼) is the signature Hangzhou dish — mild, sweet, fragrant with vinegar. Louwailou restaurant (over 170 years old) on the lake is where you should eat it if budget allows. ¥120–160/dish.

food.name: Canal-side Cafes (Grand Canal)
food.must_order: Coffee with canal view, local pastries
food.tip: The Grand Canal area (especially Gongchen Bridge area) has a cluster of design cafes overlooking the ancient waterway. Much less crowded than West Lake. UNESCO Grand Canal heritage scenery with wooden boats, old bridges and weeping willows. Excellent evening photography location.

food.name: Zhiweiguan (知味观) Restaurant
food.must_order: Xiao Long Bao, Dongpo Pork, West Lake fish in sweet-sour sauce
food.tip: Hangzhou's most famous and beloved traditional restaurant — over 100 years old. Dongpo pork here (named after the poet Su Dongpo who invented it in Hangzhou) is the benchmark. Rich, melt-in-mouth, perfectly braised. The restaurant is a Chinese heritage brand. Go for dinner — lunch gets very crowded with tour groups.

hotels.name: Four Seasons Hangzhou at West Lake
hotels.rating: 9.5/10 Trip.com | Forbes Five-Star | best hotel at West Lake
hotels.tip: The finest hotel at West Lake — pavilion-style villas set in a classical Chinese garden right on the lakefront. Private boat pier, excellent spa. Forbes Five-Star. Walking distance to Leifeng Pagoda and Su Causeway. One of the most beautiful hotel settings in China.

hotels.name: Banyan Tree Hangzhou
hotels.rating: 9.3/10 Trip.com | praised for spa and West Lake setting
hotels.tip: Banyan Tree luxury on West Lake — famous spa, stilted villas over the water, private lake access. More intimate than Four Seasons. Exceptional for couples and honeymoon travellers. Both properties are next to each other — Four Seasons slightly edges for families.

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Hangzhou
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Booking.com | good value 5-star near West Lake
hotels.tip: Best value 5-star near West Lake — Hyatt reliability at lower prices than Four Seasons or Banyan Tree. Walk to lake in 10 minutes. Good pool, breakfast praised. English-friendly staff. Solid choice for families and business travellers wanting West Lake access without luxury prices.

hotels.name: Radisson Blu Hangzhou
hotels.rating: 8.4/10 Booking.com | good for Grand Canal area
hotels.tip: Well-located 4-star near the Grand Canal — slightly away from West Lake but excellent value. Praised for spacious rooms and breakfast variety. Good metro access. A strong mid-range choice if West Lake hotels are full or over budget.

hotels.name: James Joyce Coffetel Hangzhou Xihu
hotels.chinese: 杭州西湖詹姆斯乔伊斯文化酒店
hotels.rating: 8.3/10 Booking.com | best-reviewed budget near West Lake
hotels.tip: Best budget option near West Lake — James Joyce Coffetel brand known for cleanliness and young, helpful staff. Walk to West Lake in 5 minutes. Coffee shop atmosphere in lobby. Very popular with young Chinese travellers and budget-conscious foreigners. Book ahead.

hangzhou.recommended_base_reason: Stay near West Lake for easy access to all lakeside sights

kids_attractions.name: Hangzhou Evergrande Fahua World
kids_attractions.tip: Hangzhou's main family theme park — gentler rides than Happy Valley, good for ages 3–8. The Monkey King themed section is popular with Chinese children. Better option than travelling far for older theme parks. Combine with West Lake on the same day.

kids_attractions.name: Hangzhou Aquarium
kids_attractions.tip: Compact but well-designed aquarium near the Grand Canal. Shark tunnel, jellyfish room and interactive rock pool. Best for under-8s. Very manageable size — you won't be walking for hours. Combine with a Grand Canal boat ride and lunch nearby.

xian.name: Xi'an
xian.tagline: Capital of 13 Dynasties — Where China Began

attractions.name: Terracotta Warriors
attractions.tip: Go at 7:30am opening — book 3 days ahead. Hire official guide inside (¥100) — without one, you just see holes in the ground. Pit 1 shows the army formation; Pit 2 has the kneeling archer; Pit 3 is the command centre. Friday/Saturday evening sessions (19:00–21:00) are spectacular and uncrowded.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Xi'an City Wall
attractions.tip: World's most complete ancient city wall — 13.7km, 600 years old. Rent a bicycle (¥45/hr) and cycle the full circuit in 90 mins. Sunset ride on the south wall is magical. The south gate has a working drawbridge. Autumn is best when the moat reflects gold trees.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shaanxi History Museum
attractions.tip: One of China's four major history museums — 370,000 artefacts. Star pieces: the gold Beast Head Agate Cup and Tang Dynasty musical camel. Free but book via WeChat 3+ days ahead. Free guided tours 10am and 2pm from the lobby — much better than the audio guide. Closed Mondays.
attractions.description: China's finest provincial museum — 370,000 artefacts spanning 1.15 million years of human history, including the Tang dynasty gold and silver treasures that are among the most beautiful objects ever made in China. Free but must book online weeks ahead.

attractions.name: Muslim Quarter (Huimin Jie)
attractions.tip: Xi'an's Hui Muslim quarter for 1,300 years. The Great Mosque (清真大寺) is free to enter — stunning Chinese-Islamic architecture from the Tang dynasty. For food: skip the tourist-facing main drag and eat on the perpendicular side streets. Most atmospheric after 6pm when neon and lanterns come on.
attractions.description: China's most atmospheric food street — a maze of lanes in Xi'an's Muslim Hui minority neighbourhood where roujiamo, liangpi and pomegranate juice have been sold for 1,000 years. Best at night when the crowds and the aroma of cumin and chilli are at their peak.
attractions.photo_query: xian muslim quarter street food night

attractions.name: Da Tang Bu Ye Cheng (Tang Paradise)
attractions.tip: Tang Dynasty-themed pedestrian boulevard. The famous 'Wobbly Lady' (不倒翁小姐姐) performer is at the south plaza. The Sheng Tang Mi He (盛唐密盒) interactive historical quiz show is brilliant. The lantern sky display runs after 8pm. Completely free.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Big Wild Goose Pagoda
attractions.tip: 1,400-year-old Tang dynasty pagoda. Tip from locals: don't bother climbing (steep, mediocre view) — instead watch the giant music fountain show in the north plaza at night (Asia's largest). The temple courtyards below are excellent for photography at any hour.
attractions.description: A Tang dynasty Buddhist pagoda built in 652AD to house sutras brought from India by the monk Xuanzang — the inspiration for Journey to the West. The Musical Fountain show in the plaza every evening is the largest in Asia and completely free.
attractions.photo_query: big wild goose pagoda xian tang dynasty

attractions.name: Huaqing Palace & Chang Hen Ge Show
attractions.tip: Former Tang imperial hot spring where Emperor Xuanzong and Yang Guifei's love story unfolded. The evening show 'Song of Eternal Regret' (长恨歌) is an open-air water stage performance — book mid-tier seats. The original Tang dynasty bathing pools are preserved. 5km from Terracotta Warriors — combine in one day.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-site in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Bell Tower & Drum Tower
attractions.tip: Two Ming dynasty towers marking the city's historic centre. Night views are better — illuminations are spectacular. Drum performances every 30 minutes. The underground passage between towers is free. Combine with Muslim Quarter (5 mins walk) for a great evening.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yongxingfang Food Street
attractions.tip: Food heritage street representing all 107 Shaanxi counties. The famous smash-bowl wine ritual (摔碗酒) is here — ¥5 per shot, smash the clay bowl for good luck. More authentic food and much less touristy than the Muslim Quarter main street. Also try: Hanzhong hot rice skin noodles, Zichang pancakes.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shaanxi Archaeology Museum
attractions.tip: Brand new 2022 museum — China's first archaeological process museum showing how excavations work from dig to lab to display. The Terracotta Warriors restoration process is explained in detail. Must book ahead; very popular. Harder to get into than the main History Museum.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Huashan Mountain
attractions.tip: One of China's Five Sacred Mountains with terrifying cliff-face plank walkways. Take the cable car up (¥140 return) and spend energy on the peaks. The Plank Road in the Sky (长空栈道) is optional but extraordinary. 2hrs east of Xi'an by train. Best in spring and autumn.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Small Wild Goose Pagoda + Xi'an Museum
attractions.tip: Often skipped for the more famous pagoda but more peaceful and intimate. 707 AD, partially shattered by earthquake and 'healed' — locals call it a miracle. The Xi'an Museum attached (free) has excellent Tang dynasty artefacts. Almost no tourist crowds.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-site in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Stele Forest Museum
attractions.tip: World's largest collection of stone inscriptions — 3,000 steles spanning 1,000 years of Chinese calligraphy. The 2025 north wing has holographic projection of the Zhao Jun Six Steeds. Do the rubbings workshop (¥30) — deeply satisfying hands-on experience.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: 12 Hours of Chang'an Immersive Street
attractions.tip: Immersive Tang dynasty living history — NPC actors interact with visitors throughout. Based on the famous novel/TV series. Dress in Tang costume (¥130, included with entry). Book well ahead — sells out constantly. Best time is the 3–6pm golden hour slot.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Famen Temple (Day Trip)
attractions.tip: Houses an actual finger bone relic of the historical Buddha — one of Buddhism's most sacred objects. The modern museum complex is architecturally stunning. 1.5hrs west of Xi'an. The underground vault reveals Tang dynasty imperial treasures donated by emperors to worship the relic. Genuinely moving.
attractions.description: A must-visit temple in Xi'an that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Roujiamo (Chinese Burger)
food.where: Muslim Quarter side streets — avoid tourist-facing shops on main drag
food.tip: The world's oldest 'burger' — slow-braised pork in 20+ spices crammed into a crispy flatbread. The lamb version (羊肉夹馍) in the Muslim Quarter is halal. Get it fresh from the griddle — the bread should shatter slightly when bitten.

food.name: Yang Rou Pao Mo (Lamb Bread Soup)
food.where: Lao Sun Jia (老孙家, 100 years old); Tong Sheng Xiang (同盛祥)
food.tip: Xi'an's most iconic dish — you receive two flatbreads and must tear them into pieces yourself (smaller = better broth absorption). Hand the bowl to the chef who adds lamb broth, glass noodles and tender lamb. A meditative ritual unique to Xi'an.

food.name: Biang Biang Noodles
food.where: Yongxingfang or local restaurants — ask for 油泼面 (oil-poured version)
food.tip: Belt-wide noodles nearly a metre long — each one as wide as your palm. Topped with chilli, garlic and vegetables, then smoking hot oil poured over making a satisfying sizzle. The character 'biang' has 57 strokes — the most complex in Chinese. Order 三合一 (san he yi) for the best variety plate.

food.name: Smash Bowl Wine
food.where: Yongxingfang Food Street
food.tip: Shot of grain liquor in a clay bowl — drink it, smash the bowl for good luck. ¥5 per shot. Tourist theatre but genuinely fun. The alley gets packed after 6pm. Buy 3–4 rounds and take videos.

food.name: Rou Wan Hu La Tang
food.where: Saji Bridge (洒金桥) area — morning only until ~10:30am
food.tip: Thick, gelatinous, spicy broth with pork meatballs, potato starch and black pepper — Xi'an's famous breakfast street food. Goes perfectly with a lamb roujiamo. Only at morning market stalls until about 10:30am.

food.name: Jian Shui Gao (Sweet Rice Cake)
food.where: Muslim Quarter and morning markets near the Drum Tower
food.tip: Layered steamed glutinous rice cake with red dates and red beans — a Xi'an breakfast staple with 4,000 years of history. Naturally sweet and wonderfully sticky. Best eaten warm. Morning market stalls near the drum tower have the best versions.

food.name: Xi'an Dumplings Banquet
food.where: De Fa Chang (德发长) on Bell Tower Square — since 1936
food.tip: Up to 108 different dumplings, each shaped to mirror its filling — fish-shaped for fish, boat-shaped for shrimp. Order the set banquet. More theatrical than gourmet but genuinely a Xi'an cultural experience.

food.name: Persimmon Cake
food.where: Muslim Quarter stalls and Yongxingfang
food.tip: Flat fried pancakes made with persimmon fruit — crispy outside, sticky-sweet inside. Shaanxi is China's biggest persimmon province. Best in autumn (October) when persimmons ripen. One of Xi'an's most underrated street snacks.

food.name: Muslim Quarter Street Food
food.near: Muslim Quarter (Huimin Jie), Bell Tower area
food.must_order: Roujiamo (meat sandwich), liangpi (cold noodles), pomegranate juice, persimmon cake
food.tip: Xi'an's most essential food experience — a maze of streets lined with Muslim Hui minority food stalls and restaurants. Go at night when it's busiest. Key dishes: roujiamo (shredded braised beef in crispy flatbread — China's original burger), liangpi (cold starchy noodles in vinegar and chilli sauce), persimmon cakes (fresh in autumn). The chaos and aroma are part of the experience.

food.name: Yang's Rou Jia Mo (老孙家)
food.must_order: Roujiamo with braised beef, lamb paomo soup
food.tip: Over 100-year-old institution for Xi'an's most famous street food. The pita bread is baked fresh throughout the day — crispy, charred edges, soft inside. Braised beef filling has been simmering for decades (they add to the same pot). ¥10–15 per sandwich. Get there by 11am or the best bread is sold out.

food.name: South Gate (Nanmen) Teahouse
food.near: South Gate (Yongning Men), Ancient City Wall
food.must_order: Shaanxi blossom tea, osmanthus cake
food.tip: Traditional teahouse near the South Gate of Xi'an's ancient city walls. Watch the gate's nightly light show (7:30pm) from the teahouse terrace. The city wall illuminated at night is one of Xi'an's best photo moments. Book a window seat for the light show — it's free to watch from anywhere near the gate.

food.name: Datang Everbright City Food Street
food.near: Big Wild Goose Pagoda area, Yanta District
food.must_order: Tang dynasty themed food and drinks
food.tip: The best evening food and walking street in Xi'an — a spectacular Tang dynasty-themed pedestrian boulevard with life-size statues of Tang era figures, light projections and costumed performers. The food stalls sell both traditional Xi'an food and creative Tang-themed versions. Best 7–10pm. The 不倒翁 (Roly-Poly Girl) viral performer appears nightly.

food.name: Shaanxi Cuisine Restaurant (Tóng Shēng Xiáng)
food.must_order: Yangrou Paomo (lamb broth with hand-torn flatbread)
food.tip: The definitive Xi'an meal — you personally tear the flatbread into small pieces (takes 10–15 minutes of dedicated tearing), hand it back to the kitchen, and it returns as a magnificent bowl of rich lamb broth with your bread, glass noodles and lamb. The process is part of the ritual. Tongshengxiang has been making this since 1920.

food.name: Lintong Local Restaurants near Terracotta Warriors
food.must_order: Biángbiáng noodles (手拍宽面), cold shaanxi liangpi
food.tip: Don't eat inside the tourist restaurants at the Terracotta Warriors entrance — 3x the price for half the quality. Walk 500m toward the village and find local noodle shops. The best thing to eat in the area is biángbiáng noodles — extra wide hand-pulled noodles with chilli oil and vinegar. ¥15–20 per bowl.

hotels.name: Sofitel Legend People's Grand Hotel Xi'an
hotels.chinese: 西安人民大厦索菲特传奇酒店
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com | most iconic address in Xi'an
hotels.tip: The most storied hotel in Xi'an — built in 1953, every Chinese leader has stayed here. Recently rebranded as Sofitel Legend. Walking distance to Bell Tower and City Wall. The most iconic address in Xi'an for history lovers.

hotels.name: Rosewood Xi'an
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Trip.com | Forbes Five-Star | most luxurious in Xi'an
hotels.tip: Forbes Five-Star — the finest hotel in Xi'an. Tang dynasty-inspired architecture in the cultural Qujiang district near Big Goose Pagoda. Extraordinary spa, indoor pool, impeccable service. A 20-min metro ride to the old city — trade centrality for luxury.

hotels.name: Hilton Xi'an
hotels.rating: 8.6/10 Booking.com | praised for location inside city walls
hotels.tip: Best mid-range with a prime old-city location — directly inside the ancient walls, 5-min walk to Bell Tower and Drum Tower. Good for walking to Muslim Quarter. Breakfast praised. English-speaking staff. One of the best-located mid-range hotels in Xi'an.

hotels.name: Grand Mercure Xi'an on Renmin Square
hotels.chinese: 西安人民广场美爵酒店
hotels.rating: 8.3/10 Booking.com | good value near Bell Tower
hotels.tip: Reliable Accor mid-range near People's Square — walk to Bell Tower in 10 minutes, City Wall in 15. Good breakfast with both Chinese and Western options. Praised for spacious rooms. Accor loyalty points. Strong value in a prime central location.

hotels.name: Ibis Xi'an Drum Tower
hotels.rating: 8.2/10 Booking.com | best budget location in Xi'an
hotels.tip: Unbeatable budget location in Xi'an — walking distance to Drum Tower, Bell Tower and the Muslim Quarter night market. Accor reliability. Small rooms but immaculate. Staff praised for helpfulness with directions and restaurant recommendations. Book early.

xian.recommended_base_reason: Inside or near the ancient city walls — Bell Tower, Muslim Quarter walkable

kids_attractions.name: Xi'an Tang Paradise (Kids Version)
kids_attractions.tip: The larger, older Tang dynasty themed park — different from Da Tang Bu Ye Cheng. Has an actual lake, boat rides, Tang costume rental and cultural performances. Less commercialised than the famous pedestrian street. The nightly water-light show (¥60 extra) is spectacular. Kids love the boat rides.

kids_attractions.name: Qujiang Ocean World
kids_attractions.tip: Xi'an's main aquarium — surprisingly excellent for a landlocked city. Underwater tunnel, shark pool, beluga whale viewing and seal performances. The hands-on touch pool (starfish, sea cucumbers) is very popular with young children. Located in the Qujiang cultural district, easy to combine with nearby Tang ruins park.

guilin.name: Guilin
guilin.tagline: The World's Most Beautiful River Scenery

attractions.name: Li River Cruise
attractions.tip: 83km cruise from Guilin to Yangshuo through dramatic karst landscape. The ¥20 RMB banknote scene is real. Take a 3-star boat (¥215, includes Yangshuo ticket). The Xingping section in the final third is the most spectacular. Departs 9:30am. Book 3+ days ahead in peak season.
attractions.description: The most painted landscape in China — 83km of karst limestone peaks rising vertically from flat rice paddies, the scene that appears on every 20 yuan note. The 4-hour cruise from Guilin to Yangshuo is most spectacular in morning mist when the peaks dissolve into the sky.
attractions.photo_query: li river guilin karst mountain cruise

attractions.name: Elephant Trunk Hill
attractions.tip: Guilin's symbol — a hill shaped exactly like an elephant drinking from the Li River. The moon-shaped cave in the trunk perfectly frames the river. Best viewed from the opposite bank (free). Sunrise here — the cave glows gold. The park has Tang dynasty Buddhist cave inscriptions.
attractions.description: Guilin's most iconic symbol — a karst rock formation rising from the Li River that looks exactly like an elephant drinking from the water. The 'elephant' has been recognisable for 1,300 years and the pagoda on its back dates from the Song dynasty.

attractions.name: Longji Rice Terraces
attractions.tip: 2,300-year-old terraces built by Zhuang and Yao minorities. Spring (April/May — flooded terraces) and autumn (September/October — golden rice) are peak times. Seven Stars Embrace the Moon viewpoint is the most photographed. 2hrs from Guilin.
attractions.description: 2,000 years of agricultural engineering carved into mountain slopes by the Zhuang and Yao minority peoples — the terraces spiral up the mountainside from 300 to 1,100 metres. Dawn in October when the autumn harvest turns the terraces gold is the finest view.

attractions.name: Yangshuo West Street
attractions.tip: 300-year-old main street of Yangshuo — famous as the most international village in China. Avoid daytime when tour groups arrive — go after 6pm when it transforms. The surrounding countryside is the real attraction.
attractions.description: The most beautiful small town in China — a village surrounded on all sides by karst peaks, with the Yulong River providing perfect cycling and bamboo-rafting routes through the countryside. The rice paddies and hidden villages beyond the tourist centre are worth staying for.

attractions.name: Yulong River Bamboo Raft
attractions.tip: Better than the Li River cruise for pure intimacy — two people per bamboo raft, poled by a local through perfect karst landscape. The Gong Nong Bridge section is most photographed. Go 8am before tour groups. Crystal-clear water — visible river bottom throughout.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Silver Cave
attractions.tip: One of the most spectacular limestone caves in southern China — stalactites that sparkle like silver diamonds. The natural music stone formations produce different tones when struck. 2km of illuminated passages through 12 mountains. The 'Guanghan Palace' formation is extraordinary. 1hr south of Yangshuo.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Xianggong Mountain Sunrise
attractions.tip: The best panoramic viewpoint over Li River and karst peaks — the photo you've seen everywhere. Must arrive before sunrise (5:30am in summer). Misty mornings are most ethereal. 20-minute hike. This view won the 10th International Photography Exhibition bronze award.
attractions.description: A must-visit viewpoint in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Two Rivers Four Lakes Night Cruise
attractions.tip: Night cruise through Guilin's central waterway linking Li River and four lakes. The Sun and Moon Twin Pagodas lit gold and silver are extraordinary. Book the later 9pm departure for full effect. Go on a clear night.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Impression Liu Sanjie Show
attractions.tip: Zhang Yimou's spectacular outdoor performance using the actual Li River and karst mountains as the stage — 600 performers. One of the best shows in China. Book the medium-tier seats (VIP rows 5–10). The lighting turns the mountains purple and gold.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Xingping Ancient Town + ¥20 Note Viewpoint
attractions.tip: Where Presidents Clinton and Obama stopped to admire the river. The ¥20 RMB viewpoint is 10 minutes walk through the village — the fisherman with cormorant birds still poses for photos at dawn. 1,500-year-old town, far less touristy than Yangshuo.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Ten Mile Gallery Cycling
attractions.tip: 5km cycling route through karst valleys — paddy fields, ancient bridges, bamboo groves. Rent bicycle (¥20/hr) or e-bike (¥30/hr). Moon Hill at the end has a natural arch to hike through. Early morning when mist clings to the peaks is magical.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Seven Star Park
attractions.tip: Guilin's largest urban park — seven peaks forming the Big Dipper pattern. The 1,200-year-old Qixia Temple is inside. A giant panda habitat was added. Former US President Clinton gave a speech here in 1998. Good value and far less crowded than the main sights.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Guilin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Guilin Rice Noodles
food.where: Any local noodle shop — ask for 卤菜粉
food.tip: Guilin's signature dish — thin rice noodles in rich bone broth with braised toppings. The condiment table (chilli, pickled beans, peanuts) is the key — add generously. The dry-mix version (干拌) with chilli oil and no broth is even better. Never more than ¥15 at street level.

food.name: Beer Fish
food.where: Yangshuo — Da Shi Fu (大师傅) rated highest by locals
food.tip: Yangshuo's most famous dish — whole river fish braised in local beer with chilli and tomatoes. Order it whole — one fish serves 2. Take a taxi 5 minutes out of West Street for the authentic version; tourist-facing restaurants are inferior.

food.name: Stuffed Vegetables (Rong Cai)
food.where: Countryside family restaurants around Yangshuo
food.tip: Guilin Zhuang minority speciality — minced pork stuffed into hollowed-out bitter melon, tofu, peppers and eggplant. A gentle, homely dish. Seek it at countryside farmhouse restaurants — they do it much better than town restaurants.

food.name: Oil Tea (You Cha)
food.where: Local guesthouses in Longji Rice Terrace area
food.tip: Traditional Yao and Dong minority breakfast drink — slightly bitter, savoury broth poured over puffed rice, peanuts and green onions. Locals drink 3–4 bowls every morning. The hospitality ritual of offering oil tea is an important cultural tradition. Try it respectfully.

food.name: Guilin Chilli Sauce
food.where: Local supermarkets
food.tip: The most famous condiment in southern China — Guilin's fermented chilli bean sauce. Goes on everything. Buy a jar to take home (250g jars pack well). The local markets sell homemade versions superior to commercial ones.

food.name: Bamboo Rice
food.where: Long Ji Rice Terrace area
food.tip: Glutinous rice cooked inside bamboo tubes over fire — the bamboo imparts a subtle sweetness. A Zhuang minority speciality sold at the entrance to the rice terraces. Crack the bamboo open yourself. Goes perfectly with stir-fried mountain greens.

food.name: Zhengyang Pedestrian Street Cafes
food.must_order: Coffee with karst mountain backdrop, guilin rice noodles
food.tip: Guilin's main pedestrian food and shopping street has several good cafes with outdoor seating. The karst mountains visible from every direction make even an ordinary coffee moment look extraordinary. Best in the late afternoon when the mountains glow in low light.

food.name: Guilin Rice Noodles (桂林米粉)
food.near: Any local shop in Guilin — everywhere
food.must_order: Beef or braised pork rice noodles with toppings bar
food.tip: Guilin's signature dish — thin rice noodles in a rich broth with a self-service toppings bar (pickled beans, peanuts, crispy fried dough, chilli). ¥8–12 per bowl. The key is the broth — simmered for hours with pork bones and spices. Every Guilin local has their 'best' shop. Ask your hotel receptionist for their recommendation.

food.name: Two Rivers Four Lakes Night Cruise Food
food.near: Two Rivers Four Lakes cruise departure point
food.must_order: Beer on the boat, snacks from riverside stalls before boarding
food.tip: The evening cruise on the Two Rivers Four Lakes illuminated waterway is one of Guilin's best experiences. Stock up on local snacks and cold beer from riverside stalls before boarding. The pagodas, bridges and karst mountains reflected in floodlit water are extraordinary for photography. Bring a tripod or use a phone stabiliser.

food.name: West Street Cafes (Yangshuo)
food.must_order: Fresh fruit shake, local beer, banana pancake
food.tip: Yangshuo West Street has been the heartbeat of China's backpacker scene since the 1980s. Dozens of cafes with outdoor seating under karst mountains. The famous 'banana pancake trail' passes through here. Fresh fruit shakes from Li River fruit vendors (mango, papaya, passion fruit) are extraordinary. Best evenings when the street lights up and musicians play.

food.name: Yulong River Countryside Restaurants
food.must_order: Beer fish (啤酒鱼), steamed river shrimp, rural rice
food.tip: The best food experience near Yangshuo — small farmhouses (农家乐) along the Yulong River cycling route. The signature dish is beer fish — river carp braised in local beer with tomatoes and peppers. Order with cold local beer and eat watching bamboo rafts drift past. ¥80–120 for two people including cold beers.

hotels.name: Shangri-La Guilin
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com | praised for riverside location
hotels.tip: Best luxury on the Li River — direct river views, impeccable Shangri-La service, excellent Cantonese and Guilin cuisine. 5 min from Two Rivers Four Lakes area. Walking distance to Elephant Trunk Hill. The top choice for couples visiting Guilin.

hotels.name: Guilin Bravo Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.8/10 Booking.com | consistently praised for service
hotels.tip: Long-established luxury hotel in central Guilin — consistently good service, excellent Guilin cuisine restaurant. Walk to Zhongshan Road food street and Two Rivers Four Lakes. Good for tour groups and families. Popular with SEA visitors to Guilin.

hotels.name: Guilin Waterfall Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.3/10 Booking.com | named for its indoor waterfall feature
hotels.tip: Unique hotel with a famous indoor waterfall in the lobby — a Guilin landmark. Mid-range prices with a memorable feature. Walk to Li River and main attractions. Praised for friendly staff and good location. A fun choice for families.

hotels.name: The Yangshuo Village Inn
hotels.rating: 9.1/10 Booking.com | best mid-range in Yangshuo
hotels.tip: Best mid-range in Yangshuo — traditional village house converted to a boutique inn with karst views from every room. Walk to West Street, Yulong River bike paths. Praised for breakfast, service and the stunning natural setting.

hotels.name: Merryland Youth Hostel Yangshuo
hotels.rating: 8.2/10 Hostelworld | best budget base in Yangshuo
hotels.tip: Best budget option in Yangshuo — friendly staff, karst views from rooftop terrace, right on West Street. Bike rental organised from front desk. Mixed dorms and private rooms available. The social hub for budget travellers in the area.

guilin.recommended_base_reason: Guilin city is the hub for Li River cruise, Yangshuo and Longji

kids_attractions.name: Guilin Merryland Theme Park
kids_attractions.tip: Guilin's main family theme park — suitable for younger children who can't handle the hiking. Rides, shows and water attractions. Best for rainy days when the karst scenery is obscured. Pair with the Guilin zoo next door.

kids_attractions.name: Raft Ride on Yulong River (Family Version)
kids_attractions.tip: Kids absolutely love the bamboo raft experience — it's gentle, beautiful and totally safe even for toddlers. The raftsman poles you through crystal-clear water with mountains on both sides. Bring sun protection and snacks. The 'Old Fisherman with Cormorant Birds' photo stop is a highlight kids remember forever.

changsha.name: Changsha
changsha.tagline: China's Hottest Food City & Cultural Capital

attractions.name: Juzi Island + Young Mao Statue
attractions.tip: Narrow island in the Xiang River with the famous 32m granite sculpture of young Mao Zedong. Free entry — book via WeChat first. Take the tourist train (¥10) to the head then walk back. Best early morning when mist lies on the river. Mao wrote his famous poem 'Qinyuan Spring' here.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yuelu Mountain + Yuelu Academy
attractions.tip: Enter through Yuelu Academy (¥40) — one of China's four great classical academies, 1,000 years old. The 'Aiwan Pavilion' halfway up was described in Mao's poem. Autumn red maples are famous across China. University campus buildings at the base are beautiful to wander for free.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Hunan Provincial Museum
attractions.tip: Home to the Mawangdui Han Tombs — including 2,100-year-old Lady Xin Zhui whose skin, hair and organs are still intact. The original funeral silk garments are extraordinary. Free but book 7 days ahead in peak season. Closed Mondays.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Super Wenheyou
attractions.tip: A 9-storey 1980s old Changsha street recreated inside a shopping mall — with actual 80-year-old residents selling snacks in their 'homes'. The cable car trolley between floors is the viral photo spot. Queue 2+ hours to eat; visit upper floors to photograph instead. Best after 7pm for the neon effect.
attractions.description: A must-visit arts-district in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Taiping Street
attractions.tip: Changsha's best-preserved ancient street — 2,000 years of history. The Jia Yi Former Residence (贾谊故居) is free to enter and usually empty. Less touristy than expected. Best for street food: stinky tofu, sugar oil rice balls, rice skin noodles.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: IFS Mall + KAWS Statue
attractions.tip: Changsha's tallest building with Kaws' 'Passing Through' sculpture on level 7 — most photographed spot in Changsha. The 3D heart-shaped LED facade at night is mesmerising. Free rooftop view over the city. Combine with Taiping Street and Huangxing Road nearby.
attractions.description: A must-visit shopping in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Kaifu Temple
attractions.tip: Active 1,100-year-old Buddhist temple — far less touristy than Yuelu Mountain but more spiritually resonant. Morning prayer incense fills the air from 7am. Giant golden Buddha 15m tall. ¥10 entry. Combine with the Hunan Museum (10 minutes walk).
attractions.description: A must-visit temple in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tianxin Pavilion
attractions.tip: A Ming dynasty pavilion atop the last section of Changsha's ancient city wall. Low-key and rarely crowded. Best at dusk. The view from the top looks along the old wall — you can see where the city once ended.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Meixi Lake Cultural Belt + Chinese Knot Bridge
attractions.tip: The stunning 'Chinese Knot Bridge' (中国结桥) is one of the most instagrammed structures in China. The Meixi Lake International Culture and Arts Centre (Zaha Hadid-designed) is free to walk around. Best at golden hour and night when bridge lights up.
attractions.description: A must-visit scenic in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Du Fu River Pavilion
attractions.tip: Tang dynasty pavilion where poet Du Fu once docked. The river view at night (lights 19:30–22:30) is among Changsha's most beautiful. The adjacent riverside promenade is free.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zhangjiajie Day Trip
attractions.tip: High-speed train 3.5hrs from Changsha South Station. Worth doing as a long day trip for the Avatar mountains. Book train + park ticket packages through Klook or Ctrip. Better to stay 2–3 nights if time allows.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shaoshan (Mao's Birthplace)
attractions.tip: Mao Zedong's birthplace village — his original family farmhouse preserved and free to enter. 1.5hrs from Changsha. The giant golden Mao statue facing east draws crowds at dawn. The Mao Family Restaurant serves the Red Braised Pork he reportedly loved. A genuinely moving pilgrimage site.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Changsha that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Changsha Stinky Tofu
food.where: Hei Se Jing Dian (黑色经典) on Huangxing Road — oldest and best
food.tip: Fermented tofu deep-fried until black and crispy — smells terrible, tastes incredible. Dipped in chilli sauce. The black colour comes from the fermentation brine. Locals eat it as an evening snack. Don't let the smell put you off — it's milder than it smells.

food.name: Sugar Oil Rice Balls
food.where: Jin Ji (金记) near South Gate and street stalls citywide
food.tip: Glutinous rice balls fried in caramelised sugar — chewy inside, caramel-crispy outside. One of Changsha's most beloved breakfast snacks. Buy from stalls with the longest queues. Eat immediately while hot.

food.name: Kou Wei Xia (Spicy Crayfish)
food.where: Wenheyou or specialist xia restaurants
food.tip: Changsha's most famous dish — crayfish in dried chilli, garlic and secret spices. Numbingly spicy, deeply addictive. Order extra sauce for dipping bread. Locals eat this at midnight. The gloves are decorative — eat with your hands like locals.

food.name: Chao La Jiao Rou (Fried Chilli Pork)
food.where: Fei Da Chu (费大厨) — multiple branches citywide
food.tip: The dish Changsha is most proud of — thin-sliced pork belly stir-fried with fresh green chillies. Fei Da Chu has mastered this and is the most recommended restaurant in the city by locals. The pork-to-chilli ratio must be 1:1. Order extra rice.

food.name: Duo Jiao Yu Tou (Chopped Chilli Fish Head)
food.where: Tan Zong Duo Jiao Yu Tou — famous across China
food.tip: A whole steamed fish head blanketed in two colours of fermented chilli (red and green). The fish head pulls apart silkily. The fermented chilli is complex and mellow — very different from raw chilli heat. Order extra steamed rice.

food.name: Changsha Mi Fen (Rice Noodle Soup)
food.where: Any neighbourhood noodle shop before 10am
food.tip: Thin rice noodles in pork bone broth with braised pork, pickled vegetables and chilli oil. Changsha people eat this for breakfast and again at midnight. The 码粉 (ma fen) version with slow-braised toppings is most loved. Never more than ¥20.

food.name: Mao's Red Braised Pork
food.where: Mao Family Restaurant (毛家饭店) in Shaoshan or Changsha branches
food.tip: The dish Mao reportedly ate every day — pork belly slowly braised in soy, rice wine and sugar until a deep mahogany. The fat is completely transformed — not greasy but silky. The family restaurant in Shaoshan (his birthplace) serves the closest to the original.

food.name: Wenhe You (文和友)
food.must_order: Stinky tofu (臭豆腐), crayfish (小龙虾), all local Hunan snacks
food.tip: The most viral restaurant in China 2020–2025 — a 7-storey recreation of 1980s Changsha street life with food stalls, vintage storefronts and performers. The queue is always long — book ahead via WeChat. The food (stinky tofu, crayfish) is authentic and excellent. Even if the queue is too long, the entrance area is worth seeing.

food.name: Taiping Street Night Market (太平街)
food.near: Taiping Street, Tianxin District, central Changsha
food.must_order: Stinky tofu (must-try), sugar fried chestnuts, spicy crawfish
food.tip: The most authentic food street in Changsha — Ming dynasty commercial street lined with local snack stalls. The stinky tofu here (臭豆腐, Changsha-style) is unlike anywhere else in China — deep fried until puffed, served with chilli sauce. It smells terrible and tastes extraordinary. Changsha's most defining food experience.

food.name: Mango Dessert Cafes
food.near: Central Changsha, near Hunan University area
food.must_order: Fresh mango desserts, mango bingsu, mango ice cream
food.tip: Changsha is famous as China's mango dessert capital. The local mango-themed dessert shops are extraordinary — fresh mango in every form, mango soft serve, mango layers. The Chinese brand 茶颜悦色 (Cha Yan Yue Se) tea drink originated in Changsha and is not available elsewhere in China — it's a must. Long queues everywhere.

food.name: Yuelu Mountain Foot Cafes
food.must_order: Coffee before the mountain hike, local sweet rice wine
food.tip: Several charming cafes cluster at the foot of Yuelu Mountain near Hunan University. The campus itself is beautiful — worth walking through. Autumn (October) brings stunning maple and ginkgo foliage on the mountain. Stop for coffee at a campus-adjacent cafe before climbing up to Aiwan Pavilion.

food.name: Cha Yan Yue Se Tea Drink
food.near: Multiple locations across central Changsha — exclusive to Changsha
food.must_order: Yuanyangji (鸳鸯机) milk tea, any seasonal special
food.tip: This tea drink brand is ONLY available in Changsha — it's become a major reason young Chinese visit the city. The drinks use traditional Chinese tea leaves with a modern milk tea format. Beautiful branding and packaging. Always long queues — find the one with the shortest wait. Buy extras as gifts — they don't survive long journeys so drink here.

hotels.name: Wanda Reign Changsha
hotels.rating: 9.3/10 Trip.com | Forbes Five-Star
hotels.tip: Forbes Five-Star — the finest hotel in Changsha. Contemporary design with excellent views of the Xiang River. Outstanding Hunanese cuisine restaurant. Good spa and pool. 20-min metro to Yuelu Mountain. The top choice for discerning visitors to Changsha.

hotels.name: Sheraton Changsha Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Booking.com | praised for location and breakfast
hotels.tip: Marriott reliability in the commercial heart of Changsha — walking distance to IFS tower and Wuyi Square. Excellent Hunanese breakfast with local specialities. Good pool. Praised for English-speaking staff. The easiest choice for first-time visitors to Changsha.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Changsha Riverside
hotels.chinese: 长沙湘江皇冠假日酒店
hotels.rating: 8.4/10 Booking.com | Xiang River views, good value
hotels.tip: IHG mid-range with Xiang River views — good breakfast, family rooms available. Walk to Juzi (Orange) Island. Better value than Sheraton for similar amenities. Praised for river-view rooms at reasonable prices.

hotels.name: Holiday Inn Express Changsha Wuyi
hotels.chinese: 长沙五一广场智选假日酒店
hotels.rating: 8.2/10 Booking.com | best budget near Wuyi Square
hotels.tip: Best budget option in central Changsha — Wuyi Square location puts you walking distance to the IFS, Taiping Street food market and Wenhe You. Free breakfast. IHG reliability. English-friendly staff. Excellent value for the central location.

hotels.name: Orange Hotel Changsha Wuyi
hotels.rating: 8.0/10 Booking.com | clean budget brand well-located
hotels.tip: Chinese budget chain with consistent cleanliness and good locations. Wuyi Square branch is ideal for visiting Changsha's food streets and cultural sites. Clean, functional rooms. Walk to Hunan Museum, Mawangdui and all central attractions.

changsha.recommended_base_reason: Wuyi Square area — central metro access to Yuelu Mountain and museums

kids_attractions.name: Changsha Happy Valley (Huan Le Gu)
kids_attractions.tip: Changsha's largest theme park. Best for families with kids 7+. The water park section (separate ticket) is excellent in summer. Less impressive than the Guangzhou Chimelong equivalent but convenient for families based in Changsha.

kids_attractions.name: Mawangdui Han Tomb (Kids Cultural Visit)
kids_attractions.tip: The 2,100-year-old preserved mummy is genuinely awe-inspiring for curious older kids — better than any history lesson. The silk funeral garments and lacquerware are extraordinarily beautiful. For younger kids (under 8) the preservation chamber viewing may be scary. The children's discovery corner has hands-on Han dynasty activities.

zhangjiajie.name: Zhangjiajie

attractions.name: Wulingyuan National Forest Park (Avatar Mountains)
attractions.tip: The inspiration for Avatar's floating Hallelujah Mountains. 3,000+ sandstone pillars draped in mist. 4-day pass (¥298) covers the full park. The Bailong Elevator (百龙天梯, ¥72 extra) lifts you 330m in 90 seconds — essential. Yuanjiajie has the famous 'Avatar Hallelujah Mountain' (乾坤柱). Start at 8am before fog clears.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tianmen Mountain + Glass Walkway
attractions.tip: World's longest cable car (7,455m) rises to 1,518m. Tianmen Cave is a huge natural arch that small planes fly through annually. The glass walkway clings to a sheer cliff — terrifying. The 99-bend mountain road is famous. Buy tickets online to avoid queues.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Grand Canyon Glass Bridge (Yuntian Du)
attractions.tip: World's highest and longest glass bridge — 430m long, 300m above the canyon floor. 99 glass panels between you and the abyss. Only 8,000 visitors/day — book well ahead. The bungee jump from the centre is the world's highest (¥1,980).
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Bailong Elevator (World's Tallest Outdoor Lift)
attractions.tip: Three double-deck elevators built into a cliff — rises 330m vertically through solid rock in 90 seconds. One of China's engineering wonders. The glass sides reveal the sandstone pillar forest on the way up.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Jinbian Stream (Golden Whip Stream)
attractions.tip: A pristine 7.5km valley trail alongside a crystal-clear stream through towering sandstone pillars. Wild monkeys often appear along the path. The water is shallow enough to wade. Much less crowded than the main viewing areas. Early morning when mist hangs low is magical.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tianzi Mountain
attractions.tip: The highest peak area in Wulingyuan — 'The King of Peak Forest'. The He Long Park viewpoint has the most dramatic cloud inversions. The Imperial Brush Peak resembles a giant calligraphy brush. Best in autumn. Morning cable car (¥66) saves the hike up.
attractions.description: The sandstone pillar landscape that inspired James Cameron's floating Hallelujah Mountains in Avatar — 3,000 quartzite columns rising through cloud forest mist, some over 200 metres tall. In morning cloud the pillars genuinely appear to float, and the light changes the landscape completely every hour.

attractions.name: Yellow Dragon Cave
attractions.tip: One of the largest cave systems in China — 140m deep, 10km explored, 13 chambers. Its own underground river and waterfall. The 'Dragon Palace' stalactite formations are extraordinary. Last entry 5pm.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Baofeng Lake Boat Ride
attractions.tip: A hidden emerald lake surrounded by vertical sandstone cliffs — only accessible by boat. The water reflection of the cliffs on calm mornings is perfectly symmetrical. The boat ride leads to a waterfall and temple. Much quieter than the main park.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Fenghuang Ancient Town (Day/Night Trip)
attractions.tip: One of China's most photogenic ancient towns — Miao minority culture, stilted buildings over the Tuojiang River. 3hrs south of Zhangjiajie by bus. Stay at least one night — the neon reflection in the river after dark is extraordinary.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Charming Xiangxi Night Show
attractions.tip: Top-rated evening show in Zhangjiajie — Tujia and Miao minority acrobatics, fire-spinning, traditional music. More authentic than similar shows elsewhere. Book mid-range seats (rows 8–15). Starts 8pm nightly.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Zhangjiajie that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Tujia Three Down the Pot
food.where: Local restaurants in Wulingyuan town
food.tip: The iconic Tujia dish — three ingredients (usually pork belly, tofu and cured meat) thrown in a wok with mountain chillies. The name comes from soldiers cooking quickly before battle. Hearty, rustic and delicious.

food.name: Smoked Mountain Bacon
food.where: Family restaurants across the city
food.tip: Mountain-smoked pork belly — air-dried over pine smoke for months. Stir-fried with chilli and garlic. The smokiness is deep and complex. Buy vacuum-packed as a souvenir — it travels well.

food.name: Rock Fungus & Mountain Herb Chicken
food.where: Mountain guesthouses in Wulingyuan
food.tip: Mountain black rock fungus (岩耳) stewed with free-range chicken — grows on the sandstone cliffs. Unique to this area. Deeply warming after a day of hiking. Found only on restaurant menus around the national park.

food.name: Tujia Sour Fish Broth
food.where: Tujia minority restaurants
food.tip: Fish broth fermented in clay pots with wild mountain herbs — sour, warming and slightly spicy. The mountain stream fish are noticeably sweeter than lowland fish. A remarkable dish unique to this region.

food.name: Wulingyuan Entrance Village Restaurants
food.must_order: Tujia minority claypot rice, smoked pork, wild mountain vegetables
food.tip: The village restaurants at the park entrance serve Tujia minority cuisine — the indigenous people of Zhangjiajie. Smoked pork (腊肉) cured in woodsmoke, wild fern and forest mushrooms, claypot rice. ¥50–80 per person. These are the most authentic meals you'll find. Stock up on snacks and water before early morning park entry.

food.name: Zhangjiajie City Street Food
food.near: Yongding city centre, near Tianmen Mountain base
food.must_order: Ge Tao (葛根粉糕), rice tofu, Tujia sour fish
food.tip: The city centre around Yongding has better and cheaper food than the park entrance. Ge Tao — a local dessert made from kudzu root — is a Zhangjiajie speciality. Tujia sour fish (酸鱼) is a preserved delicacy. The night market near the Zhangjiajie station area is lively and cheap. ¥30–50 for a full local meal.

food.name: Tianmen Mountain Top Cafe
food.near: Tianmen Mountain summit, accessible by cable car
food.must_order: Coffee above the clouds
food.tip: Coffee at 1,518m — the cable car summit station has a small cafe where you can drink with views over the sea of clouds. On clear mornings the cloud sea below you stretches to the horizon. The glass walkway on the cliff edge is right outside. Combine with the coachway (99 hairpin bends) and Heaven's Gate cave for a full Tianmen Mountain day.

hotels.name: Pullman Zhangjiajie
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com | best luxury near city and Tianmen Mountain
hotels.tip: Accor luxury in Zhangjiajie city — good base for Tianmen Mountain and glass bridge day trips. Rooftop pool with mountain views. 40-min drive to Wulingyuan park entrance. Praised for breakfast and English-friendly staff. Popular with package tour groups.

hotels.name: Pipaxi Hotel Zhangjiajie
hotels.rating: 8.8/10 Booking.com | inside Wulingyuan scenic area
hotels.tip: The premium hotel INSIDE the national park — wake up to sandstone pillars outside your window. Take early morning walks before day tourists arrive. Premium for location, not just quality. Essential if experiencing the park at dawn and dusk is a priority.

hotels.name: Zhangjiajie Cygnet International Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.3/10 Booking.com | good value inside scenic area
hotels.tip: Good mid-range option right at the Wulingyuan park gate — walk to the entrance at 7am. Clean rooms, reasonable prices. Good base for multiple days in the park. Convenient restaurants nearby for pre and post-hike meals.

hotels.name: Zhangjiajie Qingyuan International Hotel
hotels.chinese: 张家界清远国际大酒店
hotels.rating: 8.1/10 Booking.com | reliable mid-range in Zhangjiajie city
hotels.tip: Reliable mid-range in the city centre — good for travellers combining Tianmen Mountain (city side) and Wulingyuan (park side) on different days. Praised for breakfast and helpful staff with local transport guidance.

hotels.name: Wulingyuan Village Guesthouses
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 Booking.com average | local family-run options
hotels.tip: Dozens of family-run guesthouses (民宿) right at the park entrance — ¥150-250/night. Basic but clean, owners very helpful with park advice. The social atmosphere is unique — share dinners with other hikers, compare notes on trails. Book the ones with recent 8.0+ reviews on Trip.com.

zhangjiajie.recommended_base_reason: Stay inside Wulingyuan for early morning park access

kids_attractions.name: Bailong Elevator (Kids Adventure)
kids_attractions.tip: Kids absolutely love this — the world's tallest outdoor elevator shooting straight up a cliff face. The glass sides showing the plummeting valley are simultaneously terrifying and thrilling. Toddlers in buggies are not suitable. Kids 5+ handle it fine. The moment of emerging into the peak forest at the top is magical.

kids_attractions.name: Yellow Dragon Cave (Family)
kids_attractions.tip: The cave system is genuinely thrilling for kids — underground rivers, stalactites lit in coloured lights, and a boat ride through the cave. Much better than standard museum visits. The cave temperature is a cool 15°C year-round — bring a light jacket even in summer. Kids under 1.2m enter half price.

yunnan.name: Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La)
yunnan.chinese: 云南 (大理·丽江·香格里拉)
yunnan.tagline: Heaven's Border — Diversity of Culture & Landscape

attractions.name: Lijiang Ancient Town
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage Site — 800-year-old Naxi minority town. Go before 9am when it belongs to locals. The Old Market Square (四方街) is the heart. Black Dragon Pool (黑龙潭) behind town has the iconic Jade Dragon Snow Mountain reflection photo. Very commercial but genuinely beautiful.
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Naxi minority town where cobblestone lanes lined with wooden buildings follow a network of spring-fed canals. The view from Lion Hill at dawn before tourists arrive, with Jade Dragon Snow Mountain as backdrop, is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Jade Dragon Snow Mountain
attractions.tip: Sacred Naxi mountain at 5,596m. The glacier cable car (大索道, ¥260 return) takes you to 4,506m. Altitude sickness is common — move slowly, drink water, rent oxygen bottles (¥20) at the top. Book cable car in advance — sells out daily.
attractions.description: A 5,596-metre snow peak visible from Lijiang's ancient town — the cable car to 4,506 metres gives extraordinary high-altitude views across the Yunnan plateau and the traditional Naxi villages below.
attractions.photo_query: jade dragon snow mountain yunnan lijiang

attractions.name: Blue Moon Valley
attractions.tip: Five impossibly turquoise glacier-melt lakes at the foot of Jade Dragon Snow Mountain. The colour is surreal. The reflection of the snowy peak in still water is the most photographed view in Yunnan. Go before 10am. Included in the Jade Dragon park ticket.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Dali Ancient City
attractions.tip: Ming dynasty walled city with Bai minority culture — more relaxed and less commercial than Lijiang. Rent a bicycle (¥20/day) and cycle north to Xizhou village (喜洲) for the best traditional Bai architecture.
attractions.description: A Tang dynasty walled city at the foot of the Cangshan Mountains on the shores of Erhai Lake — the whitewashed Bai minority architecture, cobblestone streets and backdrop of snow-capped peaks create one of the most photogenic urban settings in China.
attractions.photo_query: dali ancient city yunnan bai minority

attractions.name: Erhai Lake
attractions.tip: The 256km² highland lake defining Dali's landscape. Cycling the full lake (100km, 2 days) is the ultimate Dali experience. The village of Shuanglang (双廊) on the east bank has the best lake views. Sunset from the water is extraordinary.
attractions.description: A 250km² alpine lake at 1,974m surrounded by Bai minority villages and backed by the Cangshan range — the surrounding cycling and boat routes are among the most beautiful in Yunnan. Dawn over the lake when the mountains reflect in perfectly still water is the definitive Yunnan image.
attractions.photo_query: erhai lake dali yunnan mountain reflection

attractions.name: Three Pagodas of Chong Sheng Temple
attractions.tip: Dali's most iconic image — three Tang dynasty pagodas reflected in a still pool. Go just after sunrise before tour groups arrive. The main pagoda is 1,200 years old. The museum behind has excellent Nanzhao Kingdom artefacts.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tiger Leaping Gorge
attractions.tip: One of the world's deepest gorges — the Jinsha River squeezed between 5,000m+ peaks. The 2-day hiking trail (upper path) is Yunnan's most famous hike. The Tiger Leaping Stone at the gorge bottom is accessible by a short hike. Combine with the journey between Lijiang and Shangri-La.
attractions.description: One of the world's great gorges — the Yangtze River compressed into a 30-metre channel between 3,000-metre peaks. The 2-day hiking trail along the high trail offers the most dramatic mountain-river scenery in China.

attractions.name: Songzanlin Monastery
attractions.tip: Yunnan's largest Tibetan monastery — 'the Little Potala Palace'. 700 monks in residence. Morning prayer ceremonies (8–9am) with chanting are profoundly moving. The hilltop view over the complex and surrounding grasslands is extraordinary. Cover shoulders and knees.
attractions.description: A must-visit temple in Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Pudacuo National Park
attractions.tip: Pristine alpine park with two main lakes connected by boardwalks through ancient spruce forest. Best in late May (wildflowers) and October (turning foliage). At 3,500m altitude — acclimatise before visiting. The silence here is remarkable.
attractions.description: China's first national park built to international standards — pristine alpine lakes, meadows and forests at 3,500 metres, home to black-necked cranes and Yunnan golden monkeys. The boardwalk through Shudu Lake and Bita Lake is one of the finest nature walks in China.
attractions.photo_query: pudacuo national park yunnan alpine lake

attractions.name: Shuhe Ancient Town
