attractions.tip: 5km from Lijiang — same Naxi architecture but far fewer tourists. Where Lijiang is commercial, Shuhe is residential. The spring-fed stream running through the centre is pristine. Most photographers prefer Shuhe for sunrise shots. Several excellent local cafes.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Meili Snow Mountain Sunrise
attractions.tip: The most sacred mountain in Tibetan Buddhism illuminated gold by first rays of sunrise. Clear days are rare — best chance October–November. Stay at Feilai Temple (飞来寺) viewpoint the night before. If you see it, consider yourself extraordinarily lucky.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Kunming Green Lake + Old Street
attractions.chinese: 昆明翠湖公园 & 老街
attractions.tip: Kunming's most loved park — a willow-lined lake in the city centre. Winter (Nov–Feb) when tens of thousands of red-billed seagulls arrive from Siberia is special — locals sell corn to feed them. The Wenhua Xiang area around the park has excellent independent cafes.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Yunnan (Dali, Lijiang, Shangri-La) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Over-the-Bridge Rice Noodles
food.where: Fu Zhao Lou (福照楼) or Jian Shui-style restaurants in Kunming
food.tip: Yunnan's most famous dish — a large bowl of boiling broth in which you cook paper-thin ingredients. The oil layer on top keeps the broth at boiling point. Add ingredients in order: protein first, then vegetables. The legend involves a wife carrying soup across a bridge.

food.name: Wild Mushroom Hotpot
food.where: Yunnan mushroom restaurants in Dali or Kunming
food.tip: Yunnan has 850 mushroom varieties. Some cause mild hallucinations if undercooked — hence chefs won't give you chopsticks until the mushrooms have boiled 18+ minutes. The wild chicken mushroom (鸡枞菌) in season is extraordinary.

food.name: Yunnan Goat Cheese (Rubing/Rushan)
food.where: Street stalls in Dali and Lijiang
food.tip: Yunnan's unique pressed goat cheese — pan-fried until golden, served with chilli salt or rose jam. The rushan variety is stretched into a wand and fried on a stick. Crispy outside, squeaky inside. A Bai minority tradition dating back 400 years.

food.name: Yunnan Mixian
food.where: Any small noodle shop in any Yunnan city
food.tip: Yunnan's everyday noodle — thin rice vermicelli in bright red bone broth with pickled vegetables, pork mince and chilli oil. The vermicelli is noticeably silkier than other provinces. Never more than ¥20 at street level.

food.name: Naxi Baba (Flatbread)
food.where: Morning street stalls in Lijiang old town
food.tip: Naxi minority's layered flatbread made with lard and spring onion on a stone griddle. Crispy and savoury. Sweet version with red bean paste also available. The smell of cooking baba drifts through the old town by 7am.

food.name: Tibetan Yak Butter Tea
food.where: Tea houses in Shangri-La ancient town
food.tip: Black tea churned with yak butter and salt — thick, fatty, warming and nothing like regular tea. Locals drink 10+ cups daily in the cold highlands. Accept it respectfully when offered by a Tibetan family.

food.name: Yunnan BBQ Skewers
food.where: Night market stalls in Lijiang and Dali
food.tip: Skewers of mushrooms, tofu, vegetables and meat cooked over charcoal with chilli oil and cumin. The stalls set up at dusk around both ancient towns. The wild mushroom skewers are extraordinary — order regardless of what else you get.

food.name: Shuhe Ancient Town Cafes
food.near: Shuhe Ancient Town, 4km from Lijiang old town
food.must_order: Yunnan coffee (云南小粒咖啡), Naxi-spiced cake
food.tip: Shuhe is quieter and more atmospheric than the touristy Lijiang old town. The cafes here are in ancient Naxi courtyards with Jade Dragon Snow Mountain visible from rooftop terraces. Yunnan grows excellent arabica coffee — this is the origin of China's best beans. Any local cafe in Shuhe will serve exceptional Yunnan coffee.

food.name: Lijiang Old Town Bar Street (酒吧街)
food.near: Lijiang Ancient Town, Sifang Street area
food.must_order: Yunnan craft beer, fresh ginger tea, Naxi ethnic cocktails
food.tip: The streets around Sifang Square at night are full of music — local Naxi minority musicians performing traditional songs in bars and street cafes. The combination of cobblestone lanes, red lanterns reflected in water channels and live ethnic music is enchanting. Best after 8pm. Avoid the tourist restaurants on the main drag — walk one alley back.

food.name: Dali Ancient City Cafes
food.must_order: Yunnan coffee, Bai minority cheese (乳扇), milk cake
food.tip: Dali has a deeply established expat and digital nomad cafe culture. The cafes on Renmin Road and Fuxing Road are excellent — many with views of the city gate and Cangshan mountains. Yunnan fried cheese (乳扇) is a Bai minority speciality unique to Dali — caramelised in a wok with chilli and honey. ¥5 from street stalls.

food.name: Shangri-La (Zhongdian) Tibetan Restaurants
food.must_order: Yak butter tea, barley wine (青稞酒), tsampa, yak hotpot
food.tip: The Tibetan food experience in Shangri-La is unique in all of China. Yak butter tea (酥油茶) is salty, fatty and an acquired taste — try it at least once. Tsampa (roasted barley flour) is the Tibetan staple. Yak hotpot is extraordinary — rich, gamey meat that's completely different from beef. The altitude (3,300m) affects both taste and alcohol tolerance — take it easy on the barley wine.

food.name: Kunming Bird & Flower Market (Dounan)
food.near: Dounan, Kunming, 30 min from city centre
food.must_order: Fresh flowers direct from farmers, Yunnan crossing-the-bridge rice noodles nearby
food.tip: Asia's largest fresh flower market — 80% of China's cut flowers come through here. Open 24hrs but best at 3–6am when the trading is most frantic. For tourists, early morning is when the market is piled high with flowers in every colour. The Yunnan rice noodle shops outside the market serve crossing-the-bridge noodles (过桥米线) from ¥20.

hotels.name: Banyan Tree Lijiang
hotels.rating: 9.4/10 Trip.com | one of China's top resort hotels
hotels.tip: One of the most acclaimed resort hotels in China — private pool villas with Jade Dragon Snow Mountain views, Naxi minority cultural programming, extraordinary spa. Set in Shuhe Ancient Town (quieter than Lijiang old town). One of the best hotels in all of Asia.

hotels.name: Shangri-La Zhongdian (Shangri-La City)
hotels.rating: 8.8/10 Booking.com | the original Shangri-La hotel in the 'real' Shangri-La
hotels.tip: A unique historical footnote — the Shangri-La hotel brand was named after this region. Tibetan-influenced architecture at altitude (3,300m). Excellent base for Songzanlin Monastery and Pudacuo National Park. Altitude sickness management protocols in place.

hotels.name: Linden Centre Dali
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Booking.com | award-winning heritage boutique
hotels.tip: Award-winning Bai minority heritage hotel in a restored 200-year-old village mansion. Best boutique property in Dali — clay tile roofs, courtyards, mountain and field views. American owners fluent in English. Praised by international travellers as the best accommodation in Dali.

hotels.name: Lijiang Old Town Boutique Guesthouses
hotels.rating: 8.5+/10 average | pick verified-reviewed options
hotels.tip: Staying inside Lijiang old town in a restored Naxi courtyard guesthouse — cobbled lanes, carved wooden windows, morning birdsong before tourists arrive. Prices ¥300-600/night. Pick guesthouses with 8.5+ ratings on Trip.com. Ask for rooms facing the courtyard, not the street.

hotels.name: Kunming Green Lake Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.1/10 Booking.com | historic Kunming landmark, great value
hotels.tip: Historic Kunming landmark hotel next to Green Lake Park — excellent location for the city's most beautiful area. Well-maintained 4-star at very reasonable prices. Walk to Yunnan University and the expat cafes of Wenlin Street. Best value hotel in central Kunming.

yunnan.recommended_base_reason: Lijiang Ancient Town is the emotional heart of Yunnan

kids_attractions.name: Yunnan Wildlife Park (Kunming)
kids_attractions.tip: 40-hectare hillside safari park with Asian elephants, rare red pandas, Yunnan's endemic species and a petting zoo for young children. The safari bus (¥30 extra) drives through the tiger and bear enclosures. Red panda feeding at 10am is a highlight — the animals are extremely tame.

kids_attractions.name: Yunnan Minority Villages (Kids Cultural)
kids_attractions.tip: 26 reconstructed minority villages around Dianchi Lake — kids can try traditional costumes from 26 different Yunnan ethnic groups, watch peacock dances, and participate in craft workshops. The Dai, Yi and Naxi village sections are most interactive. Best morning activity in Kunming before heading to Dali.

suzhou.name: Suzhou
suzhou.tagline: Venice of the East — Classical Gardens & Water Towns

attractions.name: Humble Administrator's Garden
attractions.tip: China's largest and most famous classical garden — 50,000m² of pavilions, ponds, rockeries and ancient trees. UNESCO World Heritage Site. Go before 9am for misty ponds. The 'Drifting Fragrance Hall' reflected in the lotus pond is the most photographed image. Book via WeChat.
attractions.description: China's most celebrated classical garden — 52,000m² of pavilions, rockery, lotus ponds and covered walkways where every view has been designed as a three-dimensional painting. The garden changes completely with each season, but June's lotus flowers are the most famous.

attractions.name: Pingjiang Historic Road
attractions.tip: 1.6km canal-side lane from the Song dynasty — stone bridges, whitewashed walls, willow trees. Spring violet wisteria over the canal is extraordinary (April). Street food here (桂花糕, 海棠糕) is better and cheaper than at tourist sites. Narrow enough for a single gondola.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Suzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Suzhou Museum (I.M. Pei)
attractions.tip: I.M. Pei's masterpiece — the 85-year-old architect's 'gift to his hometown'. The building (white walls, grey tiles, geometric forms) is as remarkable as the 30,000 artefacts inside. Free but book via WeChat 3+ days ahead. The back garden integrates perfectly with Humble Administrator's Garden next door.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Suzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tiger Hill
attractions.tip: 2,500 years of history — the King of Wu's tomb topped by a 1,000-year-old leaning pagoda ('China's Leaning Tower'). The Sword Pond (剑池) holds the legend of 3,000 ancient swords. Spring Flower Fair is extraordinary. Combine with Shantang Street (七里山塘) leading away.
attractions.description: Suzhou's most historically significant hill — the leaning Yunyan Pagoda has stood at a 3-degree tilt for 1,000 years, predating Pisa by 400 years. The artificial hill was constructed over the tomb of the founding king of Wu state who was buried with 3,000 swords 2,500 years ago.

attractions.name: Suzhou Silk Museum
attractions.tip: Suzhou has been the silk capital of China for 2,500 years — live silkworm rooms and historic loom demonstrations. The gift shop sells genuine double-faced embroidery (双面绣) which is Suzhou's most famous art form. Free and rarely crowded.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Suzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Lingering Garden (Liu Yuan)
attractions.tip: One of China's four classical gardens — more architecturally rich and enclosed than Humble Administrator's. The Fengrun Peak (冠云峰), a 6m naturally sculpted limestone rock, is the finest garden rock in existence. Less crowded than Humble Administrator's but equally beautiful.
attractions.description: A must-visit landmark in Suzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zhouzhuang Water Town
attractions.tip: China's most famous water town — 900-year-old village completely preserved with Ming dynasty houses on canals. The famous Double Bridge made it onto the RMB currency in the 1980s. Go before 9am or after 4pm when day-trippers leave. Gondola rides (¥100/boat, 4 people) are definitive.
attractions.description: The most authentic water town in the Yangtze Delta — 900 years of continuous habitation centred around the intersection of two canals, with original Ming and Qing dynasty stone bridges still connecting the banks. Arrive by 7am before the tour boats arrive.

attractions.name: Shantang Street (Seven-Mile Mountain)
attractions.tip: Built by Tang dynasty governor Bai Juyi in 825 AD — from Tiger Hill to the city. Red lanterns, stone bridges and canal boats. The night cruise (夜游山塘, ¥50, 30 mins) is beautiful. Less touristy than Pingjiang Road.
attractions.description: A 7th-century commercial waterway restored as Suzhou's most beautiful evening destination — lantern-lit canal boats drift past classical buildings while folk musicians play under covered walkways. The combination of water reflections and traditional architecture at night is the definitive Suzhou image.
attractions.photo_query: shantang street suzhou canal night lantern

attractions.name: Network Administrator's Garden Night Show
attractions.tip: The most intimate night cultural performance in China — a small classical garden with 8 performance spaces for Kun Opera, Pipa, ancient dance and shadow puppetry. Only 200 visitors per night. March–November only. Book 1 week ahead minimum.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Suzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Tongli Ancient Town
attractions.tip: Quieter alternative to Zhouzhuang — five lakes surround the town, 49 stone bridges in 0.5km². The Retreat and Reflection Garden (退思园) inside is a UNESCO World Heritage garden. Worth staying overnight when day-trippers leave.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Suzhou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Three Shrimp Noodles (Seasonal)
food.where: Zhu Hong Xing (朱鸿兴) — May–July only
food.tip: Suzhou's most prized seasonal noodle — only available in early summer when female shrimps carry roe. The 'three shrimp' topping combines shrimp meat, shrimp roe and shrimp paste. One of the greatest bowls of noodles in China. Runs out by 9am — book a table early.

food.name: Suzhou Pan-Fried Buns (Sheng Jian)
food.where: Yongheli (永和里) on Pingjiang Road
food.tip: Thicker, crispier base than Shanghai version with soup inside. Serve with ginger shreds and dark vinegar. Order 4 pieces minimum — eat in one bite. Breakfast only, gone by 10am.

food.name: Suzhou Braised Pork (Jiang Zhi Rou)
food.where: De Yue Lou (得月楼) — oldest restaurant in Suzhou, 1723
food.tip: Suzhou-style braised pork belly — lighter and sweeter than Hangzhou's dongpo pork. Nearly translucent after hours of braising in Suzhou's famous fermented sweet wine. De Yue Lou has been serving this since 1723.

food.name: Yangcheng Lake Hairy Crab
food.where: Yangcheng Lake crab restaurants — September to November only
food.tip: The most prized crab in China — hairy crabs from Yangcheng Lake. Only in season Sept–Nov. Females (roe-filled) peak in October; males (cream-filled) in November. Steam whole, eat with aged black vinegar and ginger.

food.name: Boat Snacks (Chuan Dian)
food.where: Traditional teahouses and De Yue Lou
food.tip: Suzhou's artistic dim sum — small steamed items shaped into flowers, fish, animals and vegetables. Originally served on canal boats. White rabbit and peach longevity bun shapes are most famous. More art than food, but excellent flavours.

food.name: Crab Shell Cake (Xie Ke Huang)
food.where: Old-style pastry shops near Guanqian Street
food.tip: A Suzhou street pastry shaped like a crab shell — golden, layered and stuffed with sesame paste or pork and onion. Baked in a clay oven. The oldest bakeries have been making these since the Qing dynasty. Flaky interior, addictive.

food.name: Suzhou-Style Congee
food.where: Morning markets near residential neighbourhoods
food.tip: Thicker and more refined than Cantonese congee — with local pickled vegetables, shredded ginger and sesame oil. Find it at local neighbourhood markets rather than tourist areas. A quiet window into how Suzhou people actually eat.

food.name: Pingjiang Road Canal Cafes
food.near: Pingjiang Road, Gusu District — Suzhou's most atmospheric street
food.must_order: Tea or coffee with canal view, Suzhou sesame cake
food.tip: Pingjiang Road is the best-preserved ancient street in Suzhou — 1,700m of canal-side pathway lined with willow trees, white-walled historic houses and indie cafes. The cafes here have tables beside the water with wooden boats drifting past. Morning (8–10am) is magical — empty streets, mist on the canal. A shortlist of China's most photogenic streets.

food.name: Suzhou Museum Courtyard Tea
food.near: Suzhou Museum (I.M. Pei building), adjacent to Humble Administrator's Garden
food.must_order: Longjing green tea, Suzhou mooncakes (seasonal)
food.tip: I.M. Pei's masterpiece museum has a courtyard teahouse with pond, bamboo and pebble-mosaic garden views. One of the most beautiful indoor spaces in China. Sip tea while looking at the pei-designed courtyard — architecture as art. Requires museum entry booking. The gift shop has the best Chinese design products in Suzhou.

food.name: Shantang Street Food (山塘街)
food.must_order: Suzhou-style soup dumplings, pine nut fudge (松子糖), scallion oil noodles
food.tip: Shantang Street at night (lanterns reflected in the canal) is one of the most beautiful food walking streets in China. Suzhou soup dumplings (different from Shanghai's) are sweeter and more delicate. The pine nut fudge is an iconic Suzhou sweet — ¥20/100g. The canal boat rides from here are the best evening activity in Suzhou.

food.name: Zhouzhuang Water Town Restaurants
food.near: Zhouzhuang Water Town, Kunshan (45 min from Suzhou)
food.must_order: Wanjia Rou (万三猪蹄 — braised pork trotter), river shrimp, water spinach
food.tip: The famous Wansan Pork Trotter (万三猪蹄) originated in Zhouzhuang — braised until gelatinous and rich, served sliced. Every restaurant in Zhouzhuang sells it. The river shrimp from the connected canals is extraordinarily fresh. Eat lunch here — the town is much quieter after the day-trippers leave in the afternoon.

food.name: Deyue Lou (得月楼) Suzhou Cuisine
food.must_order: Sweet and sour Mandarin fish (松鼠鳜鱼), beggar's chicken, scallion oil noodles
food.tip: Suzhou's most famous restaurant — over 400 years old, featured in Dream of the Red Chamber. The sweet and sour Mandarin fish (松鼠鳜鱼) is the signature Suzhou dish — whole fish deep fried in the shape of a squirrel, covered in sweet tomato sauce. Theatrical and delicious. Book ahead for dinner.

hotels.name: Pan Pacific Suzhou
hotels.rating: 9.1/10 Trip.com | praised for Su-style architecture and location
hotels.tip: Built in traditional Suzhou garden style — grey tile roofs, whitewashed walls, pavilion architecture. Only steps from Humble Administrator's Garden and Pingjiang Road. The best luxury experience that reflects Suzhou's aesthetic identity.

hotels.name: Anantara Suzhou Shantang Hotel
hotels.chinese: 苏州山塘安纳塔拉酒店
hotels.rating: 9.3/10 Trip.com | inside the historic Shantang Street waterway
hotels.tip: One of China's most distinctive luxury hotels — actually inside the heritage Shantang Street waterway system. Rooms open onto the ancient canal. Private boat trips from your terrace. Extraordinary setting that no other hotel in Suzhou can match.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Suzhou
hotels.rating: 8.5/10 Booking.com | best-located IHG property
hotels.tip: IHG mid-range in the heart of Suzhou's old town — walk to major gardens in 10-15 minutes. Good breakfast with Suzhou specialities. Reliable English-friendly service. Family rooms available. Best value for proximity to old town attractions.

hotels.name: Suzhou Grand Mercure Siji Shijia
hotels.chinese: 苏州四季世家美爵酒店
hotels.rating: 8.4/10 Booking.com | praised for design and location
hotels.tip: Accor mid-range with elegant contemporary design inspired by Suzhou's garden aesthetic. Walk to Pingjiang Road and Humble Administrator's Garden. Praised for spacious rooms and Chinese-style breakfast options. Good value mid-range in prime location.

hotels.name: Home Inn Plus Suzhou Guanqian
hotels.rating: 8.1/10 Booking.com | reliable Chinese budget brand, great location
hotels.tip: Chinese budget chain right near Guanqian Street shopping area and major gardens. Clean rooms, good metro access. Walk to Suzhou Museum and Humble Administrator's Garden in 10 minutes. Best budget option in Suzhou old town.

suzhou.recommended_base_reason: Old town puts you walking distance to Humble Administrator's Garden

kids_attractions.name: Suzhou Amusement Land Forest Water Park
kids_attractions.tip: Suzhou's oldest and most loved family park — part old-school Chinese amusement park, part water park. The Forest Water Park section has excellent slides and pools. Great value and authentic — not a tourist trap. Open May–October for the water section; year-round for rides.

kids_attractions.name: Suzhou Museum Children's Discovery Area
kids_attractions.chinese: 苏州博物馆儿童探索区
kids_attractions.tip: The I.M. Pei museum has a children's wing with hands-on activities — making traditional silk patterns, Chinese calligraphy for kids, and a 'junior archaeologist' dig section. Free, included with museum entry. Book the museum visit and arrive early. The architecture itself fascinates curious children.

jiuzhaigou.name: Jiuzhaigou
jiuzhaigou.tagline: Fairytale World of Coloured Lakes & Waterfalls
jiuzhaigou.bestTime: Sep–Oct (peak colour); Jun–Aug (lush green); Nov (snow+blue ice)

attractions.name: Five Flower Lake
attractions.tip: The most photographed lake in China — turquoise water so clear you can see ancient fallen trees 20m below. Colours shift from emerald to sapphire depending on light. Go before 10am. Autumn (Sept/Oct) when surrounding forest turns red and orange is the definitive time to visit.
attractions.description: The most extraordinary body of water in China — so perfectly clear you can see ancient fallen tree trunks 10 metres below the surface, the water simultaneously aquamarine, green and turquoise depending on the depth and angle of light.

attractions.name: Nuorilang Waterfall
attractions.tip: China's widest highland waterfall — 270m across, 20m drop. Most spectacular in summer. In winter the waterfall partially freezes into extraordinary blue ice formations. The central transit hub of the whole park is here.
attractions.description: The widest waterfall on the Tibetan plateau — 270 metres across and 24 metres tall, surrounded by snow-capped mountains and Tibetan prayer flags. The spray freezes in winter to create enormous ice sculptures that make the waterfall even more extraordinary.

attractions.name: Pearl Shoals + Panda Lake
attractions.chinese: 珍珠滩瀑布 & 熊猫海
attractions.tip: A wide limestone terrace where water rushes over in thousands of tiny streams — like a waterfall of pearls. Below is a thundering plunge waterfall. The nearby Panda Lake (熊猫海) with emerald-to-sapphire colour gradations is equally extraordinary.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Jiuzhaigou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Long Lake (Chang Hai)
attractions.tip: The highest and largest lake in the park at 4,000m altitude — 7km long, 100m deep. Move slowly at this altitude. The lake has no outlet and is fed entirely by snowmelt. Autumn when surrounding pine forests turn colour is dramatic. The silence at this altitude is profound.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Jiuzhaigou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Mirror Lake + Reed Shoals
attractions.tip: Mirror Lake is famous for its perfect mountain reflection in the morning before wind disturbs the surface. Reed Shoals is a marsh where autumn colour is remarkable. Go at 7am (park opening) to get the still-water reflection before wind arrives.
attractions.description: The most perfectly reflective lake in Jiuzhaigou — at dawn before any wind disturbs the surface, the surrounding snow peaks, forest and sky are reflected so precisely that photographs are impossible to orient correctly.
attractions.photo_query: mirror lake jiuzhaigou reflection forest mountains

attractions.name: Full Park Y-Route Circuit
attractions.tip: The park is Y-shaped — Shuzheng, Rize and Zechawa valleys. A full circuit takes 2 days. The park bus system is excellent — hop on/off at any stop. Going against tourist flow (start from the top, work down) gets you ahead of groups. Weekdays only for best experience.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Jiuzhaigou that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Tibetan Yak Hotpot
food.where: Local restaurants in Jiuzhaigou county town
food.tip: Mountain yak beef is far leaner and more flavourful than lowland beef. Order the clear broth base (清汤) to taste the yak properly. The local potato noodles (洋芋粉) make an excellent addition.

food.name: Tibetan Tsampa + Butter Tea
food.where: Tibetan family guesthouses in Jiuzhaigou
food.tip: Roasted barley flour mixed with yak butter tea — the staple food of Tibetan people for 1,000 years. Dense, nutty, filling. Ask your guesthouse host to demonstrate the traditional way of mixing at the table. A genuine cultural immersion.

food.name: Tibetan Bread & Yak Yoghurt
food.where: Restaurants near park entrance
food.tip: Jiuzhaigou's famous thick yak yoghurt topped with brown sugar — rich, tangy and much thicker than regular yoghurt. The Tibetan bread (手抓饼) alongside is chewy and satisfying. A great light lunch on the go between lakes.

food.name: Jiuzhaigou Hotel Restaurants
food.must_order: Tibetan barley noodles, wild mushroom soup, yak meat
food.tip: Food in Jiuzhaigou valley is limited and expensive. The hotel restaurants near the park entrance are the best option — Tibetan-influenced dishes using yak meat and wild mountain vegetables. Stock up on snacks and water in Chengdu or Jiuhuang Airport before arriving — everything is 2-3x Chengdu prices inside the valley.

food.name: Songpan Ancient Town Food Street
food.near: Songpan Town, 90 min from Jiuzhaigou park entrance
food.must_order: Horse meat sausage, yak jerky, Tibetan tsampa
food.tip: Songpan is the most interesting town near Jiuzhaigou — a remarkably preserved Ming dynasty walled town with Tibetan, Hui Muslim and Han Chinese cultures coexisting. The food is extraordinary: horse meat sausage (unique to this area), yak jerky, Muslim-style flatbreads. A much better lunch stop than the overpriced park entrance restaurants.

hotels.name: Jiuzhai Paradise International Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.9/10 Trip.com | praised for Tibetan design and park access
hotels.tip: Best luxury option near the park entrance — Tibetan-influenced architecture, beautiful design in nature setting. Early morning shuttle bus to park entrance included. Good restaurant serving Tibetan and Sichuan cuisine. Excellent base for 2-day park exploration.

hotels.name: Sheraton Jiuzhaigou Resort
hotels.chinese: 九寨沟喜来登度假酒店
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Booking.com | Marriott reliability at altitude
hotels.tip: Marriott reliability in a stunning location — good spa to relax after park hiking. Well-appointed rooms with mountain views. Breakfast and dinner recommended (limited dining options in the valley). Book the combo: hotel + park tickets for best price.

hotels.name: Jiuzhai Grassland Resort
hotels.rating: 8.4/10 Booking.com | unique Tibetan tent-style experience
hotels.tip: Unique Tibetan yurt-style accommodation — glamping in traditional tents with modern facilities. Views over high-altitude grassland. Popular with travellers combining Jiuzhaigou and Huanglong (1 hr apart). A memorable alternative to standard hotels.

hotels.name: Jiuzhaigou Intercontinental
hotels.rating: 8.5/10 Booking.com | good value 5-star in a remote area
hotels.tip: IHG luxury that punches above its price point in the remote valley — very few competing options, so prices are relatively reasonable for a 5-star. Good shuttle service to park. Praised for consistent service and comfortable rooms after long park days.

hotels.name: Zhongcha Hotel Jiuzhaigou
hotels.rating: 8.0/10 Booking.com | basic but clean, best budget value
hotels.tip: Basic but clean guesthouse right at the park — the best budget option available in a very limited market. Tibetan family-run, good local food at dinner. Perfect for travellers who just need somewhere to sleep before the early morning park shuttle.

jiuzhaigou.recommended_base_reason: Stay at park entrance — catch first 7am shuttle before crowds

kids_attractions.name: Jiuzhaigou Park Boardwalk (Family)
kids_attractions.tip: The Shuzheng Valley boardwalk is the most family-friendly section — flat, paved, stroller-accessible in parts. The shuttle bus connects all stops so no child needs to walk the entire park. The coloured lakes genuinely astound children. Altitude (3,000m+) means young children may feel breathless — move slowly, drink water. Not suitable for toddlers in arms.

harbin.name: Harbin
harbin.seasonal_note: Best visited Dec–Feb for ice festivals. Summer Jul–Aug is a pleasant second option for European architecture without the extreme cold.

attractions.name: Harbin Ice and Snow World
attractions.tip: World's largest ice festival — 2,000+ ice sculptures lit from within by coloured lights. Opens late December annually. Layer up (−15°C to −25°C) — hand warmers are essential. The 24 ice slides are the highlight. Book via WeChat; sells out in advance. Nighttime is the spectacle — daytime is pretty but not the same.
attractions.description: The world's largest ice sculpture festival — 2km² of illuminated ice buildings including complete replicas of the Colosseum and Forbidden City, all carved from blocks cut from the Songhua River. At night the coloured LED lighting from inside the structures creates an otherworldly luminescence.

attractions.name: Central Street (Zhongyang Dajie)
attractions.tip: Asia's largest pedestrian street — 1.4km of European Art Nouveau and Baroque architecture from Russia's 1900–1931 presence. Buy Madeleine ice cream (马迭尔冰棍) and eat it outside even at −20°C, as locals do. Most beautiful when snow covers the historic facades.
attractions.description: A 1.4km pedestrian street of Baroque and Art Nouveau buildings built by Russian architects in the 1900s — Harbin's most beautiful architectural heritage. At night the lit facades transform the street entirely into something genuinely European.

attractions.name: Saint Sophia Cathedral
attractions.tip: Harbin's most iconic building — Byzantine-style Russian Orthodox cathedral built in 1907. Best photographed at night when floodlit (from 17:30). In snow the scene is otherworldly. Interior functions as an architectural history museum. The surrounding plaza is free.
attractions.description: An Orthodox cathedral built in 1907 whose Byzantine dome and brick bell tower have survived every subsequent upheaval — the most photographed building in Harbin, it now houses an architectural history museum. The surrounding square fills with snow sculptures in winter.

attractions.name: Sun Island Snow Sculpture Festival
attractions.tip: Enormous snow sculptures — some 20m tall — covering the entire island. The sculptures are most impressive in morning light. Very cold here — no tall buildings to block the wind. The polar bear habitat on the island is excellent for families.
attractions.description: The companion to Ice World — hundreds of enormous snow sculptures on an island in the Songhua River, created by international teams of snow sculptors. The scale of the sculptures (some over 30 metres tall) and the level of craftsmanship make this the world's greatest snow art event.
attractions.photo_query: harbin snow sculpture festival island winter

attractions.name: Stalin Park + Flood Memorial Tower
attractions.chinese: 斯大林公园 & 防洪纪念塔
attractions.tip: A 1.5km riverside park along the Songhua River — where locals gather to skate, sled and socialise in winter. The Flood Memorial Tower commemorates the 1957 great flood. In winter the river completely freezes and people drive cars across it.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Harbin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Old Street Daowai Baroque District
attractions.tip: The most authentic old Harbin — early 20th century Chinese-European hybrid architecture built by local merchants who blended Chinese interior courtyards with European Baroque facades. Far fewer tourists than Central Street but far more character.
attractions.description: A must-visit historic-district in Harbin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Siberian Tiger Park
attractions.tip: World's largest Siberian tiger breeding programme — 500+ tigers in open enclosures. The 'walking with tigers' raised catwalk tour (¥100 extra) puts tigers below at eye level. Feed sessions 3 times daily. Go in winter when fur is at its thickest.
attractions.description: The world's largest Siberian tiger breeding centre — 100+ tigers in expansive enclosures that have helped double the world's Siberian tiger population since 1986. The tigers' power and beauty at close range is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Volga Manor
attractions.tip: A meticulous recreation of a Russian country estate — churches, manor houses, forests and a lake. In winter the frozen lake and snow-covered buildings create an extraordinary European-Russian fantasy landscape. Costume rental available (¥80). 45 minutes from city centre.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Harbin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Songhua River Ice Activities
attractions.tip: Every winter the Songhua River freezes 1m thick — locals set up ice skating rinks, sledge tracks and horse-drawn sleigh rides directly on the river. Free to walk on. Skating and sledge rentals ¥20–30/hr. Cars drive across as a shortcut. The most authentically local winter activity.
attractions.description: The frozen Songhua River becomes an extraordinary playground every winter — ice bumper cars, snow motorcycles, sleigh rides and the surreal experience of walking across a major river on solid ice. The local ice fishermen cutting through the 50cm-thick ice at dawn is one of the most unusual scenes in China.
attractions.photo_query: harbin frozen river ice activities winter

attractions.name: Northeast Bathhouse Culture
attractions.tip: Going to a Northeast bathhouse (澡堂) is THE authentic Harbin cultural experience tourists never do. Full scrub (搓澡) session, sauna and communal bath for ¥50–80. Locals come after the ice festival to warm up. Ask your hotel to recommend a local bathhouse, not a tourist spa.
attractions.description: A must-visit local-hangout in Harbin that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Guo Bao Rou (Crispy Pork)
food.where: Any traditional Northeast restaurant
food.tip: Harbin's most famous dish — double-fried pork with rice-vinegar glaze. Crispy, glossy, tangy (NOT sweet like the Southern Chinese version). Created in 1905 for Russian guests who wanted something sour. Eating it elsewhere is a disappointment.

food.name: Madeleine (Ma Die Er) Ice Cream
food.where: Madeleine Hotel stall on Central Street — only one official location
food.tip: A Harbin icon since 1906 — classic vanilla dairy ice cream on a stick. The rule: eat it outside on the street even at −20°C, as locals do. Only ¥5. Queue is always long but moves fast. The most photogenic street food in Harbin.

food.name: Harbin Red Sausage
food.where: Qiulin (秋林) department store — the original and most trusted brand
food.tip: Harbin's most famous food export — smoked pork sausage with garlic and black pepper, introduced by Russians in the early 1900s. Buy from Qiulin only — street vendors are inferior imitations. Vacuum-packed versions travel home well.

food.name: Northeast Stewed Dishes
food.where: Any family-style Northeast restaurant
food.tip: Enormous clay pot stews: pork belly with sauerkraut and glass noodles (猪肉炖酸菜粉条), Little Hen Stew with Mushrooms (小鸡炖蘑菇). Everything served piping hot on a gas burner. Completely filling. The antidote to delicate Southern food.

food.name: Russian Borsch
food.where: Huamei Restaurant (华梅西餐厅) — open since 1925 on Central Street
food.tip: Harbin has the most authentic Russian food in China — brought by White Russian refugees in the 1920s. Huamei Western Restaurant has been serving borscht and beef stroganoff since 1925. The borscht with sour cream is remarkably authentic.

food.name: Ge Wa Si (Russian Kvass)
food.where: Qiulin or Madeleine stores on Central Street
food.tip: A lightly fermented rye bread drink — between beer and kombucha, barely alcoholic, slightly sour. The Qiulin brand is the original. The national Wahaha brand tastes nothing like the real thing. Best served cold even in winter.

food.name: Central Street (Zhongyang Dajie) Food
food.must_order: Russian bread (大列巴), beer sausage (红肠), ice cream in winter (-30°C)
food.tip: Harbin's most beloved street food is the legacy of Russian colonial influence. The Central Street food shops sell: da lieba (giant sourdough bread rounds, baked in wood-fired ovens, ¥20–30), hong chang (smoked red sausage, ¥15–25/piece) and traditional horse-hoofed ice cream. In winter the ice cream stalls outside in −30°C temperatures are surreal — the ice cream doesn't melt, you do.

food.name: Ice Bar (冰酒吧) at Ice Festival
food.near: Ice and Snow World, Songbei District
food.must_order: Ice-glass cocktail inside ice bar
food.tip: During the Ice Festival (Jan–Feb), Ice and Snow World has an ice bar where everything is carved from ice — bar, stools, walls, cups. Cocktails served in ice glasses that are part of the decor. Temperature inside is −5°C even when it's −30°C outside. One of the most unique bar experiences in the world. Included in Ice and Snow World entry or visit separately.

food.name: St. Sophia Cathedral Cafe Area
food.must_order: Hot coffee or mulled wine, Russian-influenced pastries
food.tip: The area around St. Sophia Cathedral (the Orthodox cathedral at the heart of Harbin's Russian colonial heritage) has several cafes with Russian-influenced decor and menus. In winter the cathedral covered in snow against a blue sky is one of China's most dramatic architectural photos. Arrive early morning for empty square shots.

food.name: Haerbin Beer Brewery Restaurants
food.chinese: 哈尔滨啤酒博物馆周边餐厅
food.must_order: Fresh draft Harbin beer, smoked pork knuckle, pickled vegetables
food.tip: Harbin is the birthplace of Chinese beer — Harbin Beer (哈啤) was founded in 1900. The area near the beer museum has good restaurants for Dongbei (Northeast Chinese) cuisine — hearty, meat-heavy, perfect for cold weather. Iron pot stew (铁锅炖), pork knuckle, pickled cabbage stewed with pork ribs. Wash down with fresh draft Harbin beer.

food.name: Yabuli Ski Resort Cafes
food.must_order: Hot chocolate, hot pot after skiing
food.tip: Yabuli is China's most prestigious ski resort — base lodge cafes and restaurants are typical ski resort fare but the setting (birch forest mountains) is beautiful. After skiing, the hot pot restaurants at the base are excellent. Combine with a night in Harbin for the Ice Festival for a full Northeast China winter experience.

hotels.name: Hilton Harbin
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com | best luxury on Central Street
hotels.tip: Best luxury hotel for the Harbin Ice Festival — right on Zhongyang Dajie (Central Street). Walk to Ice Festival venue and all old city sights. Excellent heated facilities (essential in −30°C winters). Good Western and Chinese restaurant options. Very popular during festival season — book 6 months ahead.

hotels.name: Kempinski Hotel Harbin
hotels.rating: 8.8/10 Booking.com | praised for ice festival proximity
hotels.tip: Kempinski luxury in Songbei — extremely close to Ice and Snow World and Sun Island. The most convenient 5-star for Ice Festival visitors. Heated indoor pool welcome after cold outdoor exploration. Shuttle to festival grounds.

hotels.name: Sofia Hotel Harbin
hotels.rating: 8.5/10 Booking.com | great central location near Cathedral
hotels.tip: Named for the famous nearby cathedral — excellent mid-range right in the historic Russian colonial district. Walk to Central Street, St. Sophia, and Harbin's best European-style cafes. Praised for cosy atmosphere and good breakfast. Unique Harbin setting at mid-range prices.

hotels.name: Ibis Harbin Central Street
hotels.chinese: 哈尔滨中央大街宜必思酒店
hotels.rating: 8.3/10 Booking.com | unbeatable Central Street budget location
hotels.tip: Best budget option right on Central Street — Accor reliability, heated rooms (vital in Harbin winters), walk to everything. Free breakfast. Staff praised for helping with Ice Festival logistics and ticketing. Book 3 months ahead for January festival season.

hotels.name: Snow Town Guesthouses (Xuě Xiāng)
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 average for rated options | unique snow village experience
hotels.tip: If doing Snow Village (Xueying) as an overnight — basic but atmospheric guesthouses in the famous mushroom-roofed snow village. Rates ¥300-500/night including meals (essential — nothing else available). Pick ones with recent Trip.com 8.0+ reviews. The setting is extraordinary — worth the basic facilities.

harbin.recommended_base_reason: Central Street district is the historic heart near ice festival zone

kids_attractions.name: Harbin Ice and Snow World (Family)
kids_attractions.tip: Children are completely enchanted — walking through glowing ice castles taller than buildings. The 24 ice slides are the absolute highlight for kids. Queue management improved significantly in 2025 with online booking of slide time slots. Dress in absolute maximum warmth (−20°C minimum). Hand warmers inside gloves, face covering essential. December–February only.

kids_attractions.name: Siberian Tiger Park (Family)
kids_attractions.tip: 500+ Siberian tigers in open enclosures — kids can watch from the safety of enclosed walkways and vehicles. The tiger feeding session (¥50 extra, live chickens) is intense — judge based on your child's sensitivity. In winter the tigers are most active in the cold. Kids under 1.2m enter free.

kids_attractions.name: Harbin Polar Park (極地館)
kids_attractions.tip: Polar bears, beluga whales, walrus, Arctic foxes and penguins — a full polar experience. The beluga whale show and polar bear feeding are the highlights. Genuinely one of the best polar animal parks in China. Warm inside — good for half a day when it's too cold to be outside. Kids under 1m free.

changbaishan.name: Changbaishan
changbaishan.recommended_base_reason: Wanda Resort is self-contained with ski slopes, hot springs, hotels and restaurants — no transfers needed in winter
changbaishan.seasonal_note: Winter (Nov–Apr): skiing, rime ice, hot springs. Summer (Jun–Sep): Heavenly Lake, hiking, 22°C escape

clusters.wanda-resort: Wanda ski area, hotels, hot springs, Momoland snow park
clusters.north-slope: Heavenly Lake north approach, waterfall, underground forest, Julong hot springs
clusters.west-slope: Heavenly Lake west approach (unobstructed view), 1442 steps, alpine garden
clusters.erdaobai-town: Base town for north slope, restaurants, cheaper accommodation
clusters.yanji-city: Yanbian Korean culture, border town atmosphere, extra day if time allows

attractions.name: Heavenly Lake (Tianchi)
attractions.seasonal_note: Open Jun–Oct. Winter brings spectacular frozen lake views but main peak may close. Clear days are unpredictable — check weather morning of.
attractions.tip: China's deepest lake inside a dormant volcano crater — surreal blue water at 2,189m altitude. Choose your slope: North (most facilities, partial view, take jeep to top), West (unobstructed panoramic view, must climb 1,442 stairs), South (best view, limited capacity, book separately). Check weather THAT MORNING before heading up — cloud cover ruins the view. Altitude may cause breathlessness — move slowly. North slope ticket: ¥305 (¥105 entry + ¥200 jeep). West slope: ¥194.
attractions.description: A volcanic crater lake at 2,189 metres altitude — China's deepest lake fills a caldera with water so blue it appears artificial, ringed by snow-covered slopes. The unpredictable cloud cover means clear days are treasured and worth any amount of waiting.

attractions.name: Wanda Changbaishan Ski Resort
attractions.chinese: 万达长白山国际滑雪场
attractions.seasonal_note: Open mid-November to early April. Peak season Dec–Feb.
attractions.tip: China's 2nd largest ski resort — 43 trails across beginner, intermediate and advanced. Designed by Ecosign (5 Olympic Winter Games). 140-day snow season. Ski equipment rental and professional instructors available (national team level). Kids' section at 38°C — family-friendly. The 'Rime Trail' beginner slope through frost-covered forests is stunning. Book ski + hotel packages at Wanda complex hotels (Sheraton, Hilton, Westin) for best value — many include hot spring access.
attractions.description: China's second largest ski resort — 43 trails across a full range of difficulty levels, a 140-day snow season guaranteed by the volcanic lake's influence on local climate. The 'Rime Trail' beginner slope through frost-covered forests is the most beautiful ski run in Asia.
attractions.photo_query: changbaishan ski resort snow forest winter

attractions.name: Julong Volcanic Hot Springs
attractions.tip: Natural volcanic hot springs on the mountainside — 100+ vents, water up to 82°C. The famous 'hot spring eggs' cooked right in the boiling pools (¥10) are a must-try. Free to view, included in north slope park access. At 2,000m altitude, steam rising from the ground in sub-zero temperatures is magical. Combine with north slope Heavenly Lake visit.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Changbai Waterfall
attractions.tip: 68m waterfall fed directly from Heavenly Lake — one of the tallest in China. Included in north slope admission. In winter the water steams dramatically against snow-covered cliffs. 5 minutes from the hot springs — combine both in one loop.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Underground Forest (Dixia Senlin)
attractions.tip: Ancient larch forest that sank into a volcanic crater and continued growing — trees are 200–300 years old, hundreds of metres below the surrounding landscape. Boardwalk loop takes 40 minutes. Eerie and atmospheric — like a lost world. Included in north slope access.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Mójie Winter River Rafting
attractions.seasonal_note: Winter only — Dec to Feb for the iconic rime ice experience
attractions.tip: River rafting at −30°C through rime-ice covered forest — water splashes freeze instantly on your rubber suit. Bizarre and unforgettable. Not actually dangerous — rubber boats on slow-moving shallow water. The ice-crystal trees on both banks are the real attraction. Book in advance. Dress in full winter gear underneath.
attractions.description: A must-visit adventure in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Xueling Snow Village (Deer Farm)
attractions.tip: Feed reindeer by hand, ride horse-drawn sleds through snow forests, take Christmas-themed photos. Kids absolutely love this. The reindeer are very tame. Opens from mid-December once snow accumulates. Combine with Mójie rafting on the same day — both in the Erdaobai River area.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yanbian Korean Culture (Yanji City)
attractions.chinese: 延边朝鲜族自治州 — 延吉市
attractions.tip: Yanji is the capital of China's Korean autonomous prefecture — feel like you've stepped into Korea without a visa. Korean script on all signs, authentic Korean BBQ and cold noodles, North Korean restaurants. The Tumen River border checkpoint lets you see North Korea from 50m away. Great day in Yanji en route to or from Changbaishan. Fly into Yanbian Airport (YNJ) as an alternative to Changbaishan Airport (NBS).
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Changbaishan that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Hot Spring Eggs
food.tip: Eggs cooked directly in the volcanic hot spring pools on the north slope — ¥10 for 2. Unique experience. Taste is like a perfectly soft-boiled egg with a faint mineral tang. Every visitor does this.

food.name: Korean Cold Noodles (Yanji)
food.tip: Authentic Korean-style cold noodles in Yanji — completely different from Chinese Korean noodles. Thin wheat noodles in cold beef broth with pickled vegetables and hard-boiled egg. Best in winter when you need something refreshing after skiing.

food.name: Northeast Iron Pot Stew (Tiě Guō Dùn)
food.tip: The quintessential Northeast China dish — massive cast iron pot of braised pork, mushrooms, glass noodles and tofu. The Wanda resort area has several good restaurants. Hearty and warming after a day on the slopes.

food.name: Korean BBQ (Yanji Style)
food.tip: Genuine Korean-style BBQ in Yanji — marinated beef and pork grilled at the table, served with banchan side dishes. Much more authentic than the Korean BBQ in Chinese cities. Water market area (水上市场) is the best food strip.

food.name: Wanda Resort Hot Spring Restaurant
food.must_order: Northeast iron pot stew (铁锅炖), wild mushroom hot pot
food.tip: The resort hotels all have restaurants but the quality varies. The best is the iron pot stew (铁锅炖) concept — pork belly, mushrooms, glass noodles and tofu braised in a cast iron pot over an open flame. Incredibly warming after skiing. Wild mushrooms from Changbaishan forests are extraordinary — order any mushroom dish.

food.name: Erdaobai River Local Restaurants
food.near: Erdaobai River town, north slope base
food.must_order: Yanbian Korean cold noodles, Korean BBQ, Northeast stew
food.tip: The town at the north slope base has much cheaper restaurants than the Wanda resort — 60–70% less for the same food quality. Korean influence is strong (Yanbian Korean Autonomous Prefecture is nearby) — try the cold noodles and the Korean-style BBQ. The local mushroom markets sell Changbaishan specialties at source prices.

food.name: Yanji Korean Quarter Restaurants
food.near: Yanji City, Yanbian Korean Autonomous Prefecture
food.must_order: Authentic Korean cold noodles (냉면), Korean BBQ, kimchi jjigae
food.tip: Yanji feels like visiting Korea without a visa — Korean script everywhere, genuine Korean food at Chinese prices. The cold noodles (冷面) here are made by ethnic Koreans to the original recipe — completely different from Chinese imitations. The Korean BBQ restaurants near the Water Market (水上市场) are excellent. ¥40–60 per person for a full meal.

hotels.name: Wanda Sheraton Changbaishan Resort
hotels.chinese: 万达长白山喜来登度假酒店
hotels.tip: The flagship ski-in/ski-out property. Many room rates include hot spring access and ski lift tickets. Mountain view rooms are stunning. Book well in advance for winter weekends.

hotels.name: Wanda Westin Changbaishan Resort
hotels.chinese: 万达长白山威斯汀度假酒店
hotels.tip: Directly adjacent to Sheraton in the Wanda complex. More intimate, slightly smaller. Both properties share access to Hanashan Hot Spring.

hotels.name: Swissôtel Resort Changbaishan
hotels.tip: Swiss-branded resort with excellent panoramic views of Changbaishan peaks. Popular with families. Indoor pool, spa, kids' area.

hotels.name: Erdaobai River Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Town guesthouses (民宿) are 60–70% cheaper than Wanda resorts. Good base for north slope day trips. Average ¥250/night for double room. Communal atmosphere — meet local guides and other travellers.

hotels.name: The View Changbai Mountain MGallery
hotels.tip: MGallery boutique within the Wanda complex. Smaller and more design-focused than the big 5-stars. Often better value. Luneng ski resort access (slightly smaller than Wanda's slopes but less crowded).

hotels.name: Park Hyatt Changbaishan
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Trip.com | best luxury in Wanda Resort area
hotels.tip: The most acclaimed luxury property in the Wanda resort complex — Park Hyatt elegance with mountain views, world-class spa and ski-in/ski-out access. Praised for immaculate service and beautiful design. Book package rates including hot spring and ski lift access for best value.

hotels.name: Wanda Vista Changbaishan
hotels.rating: 8.9/10 Booking.com | Wanda's premium tier
hotels.tip: Wanda's premium hotel brand within the resort — slightly smaller and more intimate than Sheraton and Westin. Very good value for the quality. Pool with mountain views, excellent ski equipment rental service. Praised for smooth check-in and friendly staff.

hotels.name: Jinyue Hot Spring Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.4/10 Booking.com | hot spring access included in room rate
hotels.tip: Mid-range hotel with genuine volcanic hot spring access included — the best value hot spring experience near Changbaishan. Simple rooms but the hot spring pools (indoor and outdoor) are the attraction. Good base for north slope Heavenly Lake day trip.

hotels.name: Erdaobai River Boutique Guesthouses
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 average for good options
hotels.tip: Town guesthouses at 60-70% less than Wanda resort prices. Communal atmosphere — share tips with guides and other travellers. Pick ones with recent 8.0+ Trip.com ratings. Average ¥250/night. Walk to local restaurants and shuttle buses to north slope.

hotels.name: Swissôtel Resort Changbaishan
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Trip.com | praised for Swiss service quality
hotels.tip: Swiss hotel management quality at mid-range prices — more intimate than the big 5-stars, panoramic Changbaishan mountain views, excellent buffet breakfast. Good value in the resort for the quality of service received. Indoor pool and good ski storage facilities.

sanya.name: Sanya
sanya.recommended_base_reason: Yalong Bay has the best beaches, clearest water and highest concentration of 5-star resorts
sanya.seasonal_note: Nov–Apr is peak season — dry, sunny, 25–30°C. Jul–Sep has typhoon risk and summer crowds. May–Jun and Oct are shoulder season — good value.

clusters.haitang-bay: Atlantis, Wuzhizhou Island ferry, duty-free mall, 68 Global Food Street
clusters.yalong-bay: Best public beach, 5-star hotel strip (Ritz, Hilton, St Regis), forest park, rose valley
clusters.dadonghai: Budget beach, water sports, backpacker vibe, convenient for city centre
clusters.sanya-bay: Coconut promenade, sunset viewing, local restaurants, more affordable
clusters.tianya-haijiao: Iconic coastal cultural landmark, scenic drive west of city

attractions.name: Wuzhizhou Island
attractions.tip: Known as 'China's Maldives' — 5A national scenic area. Crystal clear water with 10–30m visibility, pristine coral reefs, 4 dive sites. Take the 15-minute ferry from Haitang Bay pier (early — 8am ferry). Activities: PADI diving (¥680+), snorkelling, parasailing, banana boat, helicopter ride. Electric cart tour covers the island in 1hr. Famous 'Lover's Bridge' and 'Sunrise Rock' photo spots. Kids under 1.2m free. Note: 70+ adults require liability waiver to board ferry.
attractions.description: China's clearest coastal water — 10-30 metre visibility, pristine coral reefs and four world-class PADI dive sites surrounding a small island accessible by 15-minute ferry. The 'Lover's Bridge' connecting two rock formations at the island's tip has been a marriage proposal destination since the island opened.
attractions.photo_query: wuzhizhou island sanya diving coral reef

attractions.name: Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark
attractions.tip: Massive waterpark attached to Atlantis Resort — slides, wave pools, lazy river, dedicated toddler splash zone. The 'Aquaventure' main slide complex includes the 'Leap of Faith' (near-vertical drop). Indoor sections work year-round. The resort's 'Lost Chambers Aquarium' is separately ticketed (¥120) and excellent for younger kids. Book combo ticket (waterpark + aquarium) for best value. Non-Atlantis hotel guests can purchase day access.
attractions.description: A must-visit theme-park in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Yalong Bay Beach
attractions.chinese: 亚龙湾国家旅游度假区
attractions.tip: Sanya's finest beach — 7.5km of white coral sand, water visibility 9m+, lifeguards on duty. The public beach section is free. Flanked by 5-star hotels (Ritz-Carlton, Hilton, Sheraton). Water sports at designated spots: jetski ¥200/15min, parasailing ¥300. Best morning beach in Sanya — arrive before 9am for empty sand. Afternoon gets crowded. Combine with Tropical Forest Park (¥158) for a full day.
attractions.description: Sanya's finest beach — 7.5km of white coral sand with water visibility of 9 metres, flanked by the best concentration of five-star resort hotels in China. The combination of tropical water quality and reliable sunshine from November to April makes this China's undisputed beach capital.
attractions.photo_query: yalong bay beach sanya tropical resort

attractions.name: Nanshan Cultural Tourism Zone
attractions.tip: Home to the 108m ocean Guanyin statue — one of the tallest statues in the world, standing on a platform in the sea. Stunning at sunrise or sunset. Temple complex includes Nanshan Temple (authentic, not touristy), Longevity Culture Garden and vegetarian restaurant (surprisingly excellent). 30 min west of city centre. Kids fascinated by the massive statue scale. Combine with Tianya Haijiao on the same western day trip.
attractions.description: The most dramatic religious statue in China — a 108-metre white jade Guanyin stands on a platform in the sea at the edge of China, visible from 10km away. The surrounding Nanshan Temple complex dating from the Han dynasty is one of China's most sacred Buddhist sites.

attractions.name: Tianya Haijiao (End of the Earth)
attractions.tip: Iconic coastal landmark where two massive inscribed boulders read 'Tianya' and 'Haijiao' (Edge of the Sky, Corner of the Sea). Romanticised in Chinese poetry and culture for centuries — couples see it as a declaration of eternal love. The crooked coconut tree nearby is now more famous than the rocks (always has a queue for photos). 40-min walk along scenic coast. Combine with Nanshan on same day.
attractions.description: The legendary 'End of the World' — two enormous granite boulders inscribed with characters meaning 'Edge of the Sky' and 'Rim of the Sea' at Sanya's southwestern tip. A classical metaphor for the most remote place imaginable has been physically real here for 1,300 years.
attractions.photo_query: tianya haijiao sanya granite rock sea

attractions.name: Yalong Bay Tropical Forest Park
attractions.chinese: 亚龙湾热带天堂森林公园
attractions.tip: Filming location for several famous Chinese films. China's only all-sea-view glass walkway. Zip line through tropical forest. Cable car rides. The glass walkway section is genuinely scary and spectacular. Best morning activity in Yalong Bay. Combine with beach afternoon. Kids love the zip line (minimum height applies).
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Haitang Bay Duty Free Mall
attractions.chinese: 海棠湾三亚国际免税城
attractions.tip: World's largest duty-free mall — massive discounts on cosmetics, perfume, spirits, watches, handbags. No customs forms needed as a Chinese domestic visitor — just show your ID. SEA visitors on a Chinese tourist visa can also shop. Evenings are best — cooler and beautifully lit. The 68 Global Food Street next door has excellent international dining options.
attractions.description: A must-visit shopping in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Hou Hai (Back Sea) Surf Village
attractions.tip: China's #1 surf destination — consistent waves, professional instructors, relaxed village vibe. Called 'China's Hawaii'. Beginner lessons ¥200 (equipment + 2hr lesson). More bohemian atmosphere than resort beaches — colourful cafes, board rental shops, local fishermen. Best waves Oct–Mar. In summer, wakesurf (indoor cable system) is popular. A complete contrast to the resort strip.
attractions.description: A must-visit adventure in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Sanya Qiangujing Cultural Park
attractions.tip: Large-scale outdoor cultural performance — 'Sanya Through the Ages' show blends Li minority culture, maritime Silk Road history and ancient Hainan traditions. Massive stadium, spectacular light shows, acrobatics and traditional dance. Evening show starts at 7:30pm. Kids love the spectacle. The park around the theatre also has a water park and children's playground (daytime). One of Sanya's best evening activities.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Sanya that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Fresh Seafood (Market Cook-Up)
food.chinese: 海鲜市场 自选加工
food.tip: Buy your own seafood from Sanya's wet markets (Jiefang Road market) then pay a nearby restaurant to cook it (加工费 ¥30–50). Best value seafood in Sanya. ALWAYS confirm price per jin (500g) before agreeing to anything. Popular: crab, prawns, abalone, grouper.

food.name: Coconut Chicken Hot Pot
food.tip: Hainan's signature dish — fresh whole coconut cracked at your table, juice becomes the broth, young chicken cooked inside. Light, sweet and fragrant. Completely different from Sichuan hot pot. Every restaurant in Sanya does this — price varies wildly. Ask locals to recommend a non-tourist one.

food.name: Wenchang Chicken Rice
food.tip: Hainan's answer to Singapore chicken rice — free-range chicken steamed then served with rice cooked in chicken broth, with ginger-scallion sauce. Light and clean flavour. SEA visitors immediately recognise it. Wenchang is the origin city (2hrs north) but Sanya has many excellent versions.

food.name: Atlantis Lost Chambers Cafe
food.must_order: Shark-blue latte, tropical fruit cocktail
food.tip: Coffee surrounded by massive aquarium tanks containing sharks and rays. Atlantis's underwater aquarium dining is one of China's most unusual food experiences. The blue ambient light and marine life create extraordinary photos. Accessible with Atlantis resort day pass or aquarium ticket.

food.name: Coconut Chicken Hot Pot Restaurants
food.near: Throughout Sanya — ask hotel for best non-tourist option
food.must_order: Fresh coconut cracked at table, chicken, dipping sauce
food.tip: Hainan's signature dish — a fresh whole coconut cracked at your table, the juice becomes the cooking broth, the young chicken cooks inside. Light, sweet and utterly unique. The freshly cracked coconut fragrance is amazing. Price varies enormously — avoid tourist restaurants near the main beach strip. Ask hotel staff for their local favourite.

food.name: Houhai Village Surf Cafes
food.must_order: Post-surf cold brew, fresh açaí bowl, tropical smoothie
food.tip: Houhai is China's surf capital and has developed a genuine surf cafe culture — colorful shacks and boutique cafes overlooking the beach. The vibe is closest to Bali or Hawaii of anywhere in China. Fresh tropical fruit, cold brew coffee, surf boards everywhere. Best late morning after the first surf session.

food.name: Wenchang Chicken Rice Restaurants
food.near: Any local area in Sanya — ask locals
food.must_order: Wenchang chicken rice — steamed chicken, ginger-scallion sauce, broth-cooked rice
food.tip: Hainan chicken rice is the ancestor of Singapore chicken rice — lighter, more fragrant, with better dipping sauces. The best is found in local coffee shops (not tourist restaurants) around Jiefang Road market area. SEA visitors immediately recognise the format and usually find the Hainanese original superior. ¥25–40 per person.

food.name: 68 Global Food Street
food.near: Adjacent to Duty-Free Mall, Haitang Bay
food.must_order: Browse the stalls — everything from Thai to Cantonese to Japanese
food.tip: Massive international food street next to the duty-free mall — dozens of stalls and restaurants covering global cuisines. Good for families with picky eaters. Evening atmosphere is lively with outdoor seating, live music and food stalls lighting up. Best visited after duty-free shopping.

hotels.name: Atlantis Sanya
hotels.tip: The destination resort — waterpark access included, multiple beach clubs, world-class dining. Book the Aquaventure view rooms. Book 3+ months ahead for peak season (Dec–Feb).

hotels.name: The Ritz-Carlton Sanya Yalong Bay
hotels.tip: Best location on Yalong Bay — direct beach access, excellent pool complex. More low-key than Atlantis. Better for couples and families who want beach over waterpark.

hotels.name: Club Med Sanya
hotels.tip: All-inclusive — food, drinks, sports and kids' club all included. Best for families with young children — dedicated kids' programmes. Saves worrying about meals. Particularly popular with SEA visitors.

hotels.name: Dadonghai Budget Hotels
hotels.tip: Multiple budget options near Dadonghai beach — ¥300–500/night. Walk to beach in 5 minutes. More local atmosphere, cheaper food nearby. Good base for budget travellers.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Sanya Haitang Bay
hotels.chinese: 三亚海棠湾皇冠假日酒店
hotels.tip: Excellent value 5-star in Haitang Bay — walking distance from duty-free, Atlantis day pass available. Better value than Atlantis itself for families who don't need the full resort.

hotels.name: Rosewood Sanya
hotels.rating: 9.4/10 Trip.com | one of China's top beach resort hotels
hotels.tip: One of the finest resort hotels in China — private beach villas, spectacular sea views, extraordinary butler service. Rosewood's most acclaimed China property. Multiple dining options. The best pure-luxury beach experience in Sanya. Book direct for best rates.

hotels.name: Ritz-Carlton Sanya Yalong Bay
hotels.chinese: 三亚亚龙湾丽思卡尔顿酒店
hotels.rating: 9.1/10 Trip.com | Forbes Five-Star
hotels.tip: The finest hotel directly on Yalong Bay beach — Forbes Five-Star, private beach section, excellent kids' facilities. Better for families than Rosewood. Great breakfast spread. The most awarded beach resort in Yalong Bay.

hotels.name: MGM Grand Sanya
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Booking.com | excellent value 5-star in Yalong Bay
hotels.tip: Good value 5-star on Yalong Bay — praised for spacious rooms, large pools and beach access. More affordable than Ritz-Carlton next door. Excellent for families. Good for booking late when Ritz and Atlantis are full or over budget.

hotels.name: Sanya Phoenix Airport Hotel
hotels.rating: 8.0/10 Booking.com | convenient for arrivals and departures
hotels.tip: Clean, functional budget hotel 5 min from Sanya airport — excellent if arriving late or departing early. Not a tourist hotel but does the job perfectly for short stays or transit nights. Free airport shuttle. Book for transit nights only.

hotels.name: Dadonghai Budget Hotels
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 average | multiple verified options in same area
hotels.tip: Multiple budget hotels cluster around Dadonghai beach — ¥300-500/night, walk to the beach in 5 minutes. More local atmosphere, cheaper restaurants nearby. Pick any with recent Trip.com 8.0+ reviews. Good for budget travellers who want beach access.

xiamen.name: Xiamen
xiamen.recommended_base_reason: Siming district puts you between Gulangyu ferry, Nanputuo temple, Xiamen University and Huandao Road cycling — the core of everything
xiamen.seasonal_note: Spring (Mar–May) and autumn (Sep–Nov) are ideal. Summer is hot and humid with occasional typhoons. January is fine but coolest.

clusters.gulangyu-island: Historic colonial island — mansions, piano museum, seafood snacks, no cars. 20 min ferry from Xiamen
clusters.siming-south: Nanputuo Temple, Xiamen University, Shapowei old harbour, Zengcuoan village, Huandao cycling road
clusters.zhongshan-road: Old shopping street, street food, 8th Market (Ba Shi), colonial architecture
clusters.jimei: Jimei University Village (Jiaogeng Memorial), Jimei Bridge, day trip easy from city
clusters.tulou-day-trip: Fujian earthen castles — 2.5hr from Xiamen. UNESCO. Hakka culture. Standalone day trip

attractions.name: Gulangyu Island
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage site — car-free island of colonial mansions, piano music (highest piano density in China), boutique cafes and extraordinary seafood snacks. Take the tourist ferry (¥35 includes return) from International Cruise Terminal — NOT the local ferry. Key spots: Piano Museum (¥50), Dragon Head Road snack street, 'Most Beautiful Corner' photo spot, Lin Qiaozhi Memorial Garden (Gynecologist hero), Port Jiahou beach. Walk everywhere — no bikes or vehicles. Morning is essential — gets very crowded by midday. Budget 4–5hrs.
attractions.description: A car-free UNESCO World Heritage island where colonial mansions from 13 countries still stand amid banana trees and bougainvillea — the highest concentration of grand pianos per capita of anywhere in the world. Walk the quiet lanes at dawn before the 50,000 daily visitors arrive.

attractions.name: Nanputuo Temple
attractions.tip: Famous Tang dynasty Buddhist temple at the foot of Wulao Peak — free entry. Beautiful multi-layered architecture, lotus ponds, large incense urns. The Buddhist vegetarian restaurant inside is excellent (try the creative tofu dishes — ¥30–80). Climb the rocky hillside behind the temple for panoramic views of the bay and Xiamen University. Must book via WeChat ahead — limited daily capacity. Walk 5 mins to Xiamen University gate.
attractions.description: A Tang dynasty Buddhist temple at the foot of Wulao Peak — the free temple grounds contain beautiful classical Chinese architecture, lotus ponds and a rocky hillside with panoramic views of Xiamen Bay. The famous vegetarian restaurant inside is one of China's most celebrated.

attractions.name: Xiamen University Campus
attractions.tip: Called 'China's most beautiful university' — stunning mix of classical Minnan architecture and modern buildings, surrounded by the sea. The famous芙蓉隧道 (Furong Tunnel) has 500m of student murals — best photo spot. Book entry via official WeChat mini-program — visitor numbers strictly controlled (weekdays easier). Enter through South Gate near Nanputuo. The campus lake and Lotus Road are especially scenic in spring.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Zengcuoan Village
attractions.tip: Original fishing village turned artsy neighbourhood — colourful buildings, indie cafes, bookshops, bars and hostels. Much more authentic than Gulangyu's tourist strip. Called 'China's most bohemian village'. Peaceful in the day, transforms into a lively night market with local snacks and live music. The beachfront boardwalk at sunset is beautiful. Walk here along Huandao cycling road from Xiamen University.
attractions.description: Xiamen's most beloved village turned creative neighbourhood — a beachfront village whose fishermen's houses now contain indie cafes, boutiques and bars, while the sandy beach boardwalk at sunset is the most romantic spot in Xiamen. Much more authentic than the polished Gulangyu tourist experience.

attractions.name: Huandao Road Cycling
attractions.tip: China's most beautiful coastal cycling road — 43km total but the 12km stretch from Xiamen University to Zengcuoan is the classic route. Rent bikes at Bai Cheng Beach (¥30/hr) — electric bikes available. Passes Yanwu Bridge (world's longest across-sea railway-road bridge), the famous 'One Country Two Systems' sign, and multiple beach access points. Morning is best — avoid midday heat. Kids 6+ comfortable on adult bikes. Stunning views in both directions.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Fujian Tulou Earthen Castles
attractions.tip: UNESCO World Heritage — massive circular Hakka communal earth buildings, some housing hundreds of families for 700+ years. The 'King of Tulou' Chengqilou holds a Guinness record. The best cluster is Gaobei (高北土楼群). Drone footage location for the animated film 'Big Fish & Begonia'. Takes a full day from Xiamen — book a tour or hire a driver (¥400-500 round trip for 4 people, much better than bus). Kids are fascinated by the 'castle' structure. An aerial view from the hillside makes the circular design click — don't miss it.
attractions.description: The most extraordinary vernacular architecture in China — circular Hakka communal earth buildings up to 5 storeys tall, some housing hundreds of families for 700 years. The aerial view that reveals the perfect circles surrounded by rice paddies inspired the animated film Big Fish and Begonia.
attractions.photo_query: fujian tulou earthen castle hakka circular

attractions.name: Jimei University Village
attractions.tip: Founded by overseas Chinese philanthropist Tan Kah Kee — beautifully preserved campus blending Minnan traditional and Western architectural styles. Bright red tilework, classical Chinese rooflines. The memorial to Tan Kah Kee is touching — a man who donated his entire fortune to build schools for poor Chinese children. Sunset here over Jimei Bridge is excellent. Combine with Jimei seafood market for dinner.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Shapowei Old Harbour District
attractions.tip: Xiamen's last surviving working harbour — old fishing boats, weathered warehouses, street art and creative cafes. Less known than Zengcuoan but more genuinely authentic. Artists and architects have moved in alongside old fishermen. The container-style art galleries and coffee shops are excellent. Walk along the seawall as the sun goes down. 15-min walk from Nanputuo Temple.
attractions.description: A must-visit local in Xiamen that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: 8th Market (Ba Shi Seafood)
food.tip: Kaiyuan Road No.8 Market — Xiamen's most authentic wet market where locals buy seafood. Get there by 8am. Buy fresh sea snails, oysters, razor clams, and eat at the stalls inside. A chaotic, wonderful experience. Much cheaper than tourist restaurants. The 土笋冻 (earthworm jelly) — a Xiamen delicacy — is best found here.

food.name: Gulangyu Snack Street
food.tip: Dragon Head Road on Gulangyu is lined with legendary snack shops. Must-try: 鱼丸 (fish balls), 沙茶面 (satay noodle soup), 花生汤 (peanut soup), 土笋冻 (savoury jelly). The Zhang Sanfeng milk tea (张三疯) is the island's most famous — always a queue. Buy before noon before popular items sell out.

food.name: Satay Noodles (Sha Cha Mian)
food.tip: Xiamen's signature noodle dish — thin egg noodles in a rich, fragrant satay-based broth with pork offal, tofu and prawns. The satay in Fujian came via Southeast Asia. SEA visitors often recognise the influence. Best at old-school hole-in-wall shops near Nanputuo or Zhongshan Road, not tourist spots.

food.name: Nanputuo Vegetarian Restaurant
food.tip: Inside Nanputuo Temple — creative Buddhist vegetarian cuisine presented beautifully. Dishes named poetically after Buddhist concepts. The mock-meat dishes are extraordinary. Book ahead — always full. One of China's most famous vegetarian restaurants. Excellent for non-meat eaters.

food.name: Gulangyu Dragon Head Road Snacks
food.near: Dragon Head Road (Longtou Lu), Gulangyu Island
food.must_order: Salty fish balls (鱼丸), satay noodle soup (沙茶面), peanut soup, oyster omelette
food.tip: Gulangyu's main food strip — buy snacks and eat while walking. The island specialties: fish balls in clear broth (bouncy, fresh), satay-based noodle soup (Fujian's Southeast Asian influence is clear), peanut soup (hot, sweet, rich). Zhang Sanfeng (张三疯) milk tea always has a queue — join it. Buy before noon before the best items sell out.

food.name: Only One Coffee Xiamen
food.near: Near Gulangyu ferry terminal or Zengcuoan village
food.must_order: Couple set — drinks, snack platter and dessert
food.tip: The most photogenic cafe in Xiamen — viral on Xiaohongshu (RED) for its beautiful decor and photo spots. Multiple Instagram-worthy corners throughout. The couple set is popular. Check their current location as they may have moved or opened new branches since 2025.
