food.name: Nanputuo Buddhist Vegetarian Restaurant
food.must_order: Creative tofu dishes, seasonal vegetarian menu
food.tip: One of China's most famous vegetarian restaurants inside a 1,000-year-old Buddhist temple — dishes are named after Buddhist concepts and presented beautifully. The mock-meat dishes are extraordinary. Book ahead — always full for lunch. ¥30–80 per dish. The setting (temple garden) is as remarkable as the food. Walk to Xiamen University gate afterwards.

food.name: Zengcuoan Night Market
food.must_order: Oyster omelette (蚵仔煎), seafood, local beer
food.tip: The best evening experience in Xiamen — the village transforms into a vibrant night market after 7pm. Local seafood stalls, indie bars and live music. Much more authentic than Gulangyu's tourist strip. The beachfront boardwalk at the edge of the village is beautiful at night. Walk here from Xiamen University along the coastal path.

food.name: Shapowei Old Harbour Cafes
food.must_order: Iced coffee, local oyster toast, passionfruit tea
food.tip: Xiamen's last working harbour turned artsy neighbourhood. The container-style art gallery cafes have harbour views and are much less crowded than Gulangyu. Visit late afternoon when the low light hits the old fishing boats. The combination of weathered boats, colourful murals and artisan coffee is very Xiamen.

hotels.name: Sofitel Xiamen On the Waterfront
hotels.tip: Best central location — harbour views, walking distance to Gulangyu ferry and old town. Excellent breakfast.

hotels.name: Gulangyu Boutique Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Staying ON Gulangyu is magical — total peace, no cars, waking up to birdsong and distant piano. Book well ahead. Guesthouses are small and fill quickly. Night-time island with no day-trippers is a completely different experience.

hotels.name: Marriott Hotel Xiamen
hotels.tip: Near Nanputuo and Xiamen University. Good value 5-star with sea views from upper floors.

hotels.name: Zengcuoan Boutique Hostels
hotels.tip: The village itself has dozens of charming guesthouses at ¥150–300/night. Sea views from some rooftop rooms. Best for solo travellers and couples who want local atmosphere over hotel amenities.

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Xiamen
hotels.tip: Modern 5-star on Haitang Bay — good for families wanting beach access in Xiamen. Slightly out of the main tourist zone but has excellent facilities.

hotels.name: Angsana Gulangyu Xiamen
hotels.chinese: 厦门鼓浪屿昂撒纳酒店
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Trip.com | only luxury hotel ON Gulangyu Island
hotels.tip: The only 5-star hotel actually on Gulangyu Island — wake up to the island after all day tourists have left. Colonial mansion conversion, spa, rooftop sea views. The most memorable accommodation in Xiamen. Book months ahead — limited rooms.

hotels.name: Crowne Plaza Xiamen
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Booking.com | best-located mainland luxury hotel
hotels.tip: IHG luxury with excellent Gulangyu ferry access — 15 min to ferry terminal. Very good breakfast, large pool. Praised for English-speaking staff and smooth operations for international guests. A strong choice if Gulangyu guesthouses are full.

hotels.name: Wyndham Grand Xiamen Haicang
hotels.chinese: 厦门海沧温德姆至尊豪廷酒店
hotels.rating: 8.5/10 Booking.com | praised for price-to-quality
hotels.tip: Good value 5-star with Xiamen Bay views — slightly further from old town but Metro Line 6 connection. Praised for spacious rooms, breakfast quality and helpful staff. Good option for families who want space and views at mid-range prices.

hotels.name: Marco Polo Xiamen
hotels.rating: 8.4/10 Booking.com | reliable 4-star with good location
hotels.tip: Reliable Marco Polo 4-star with good transport connections to Gulangyu ferry and main attractions. Good breakfast, efficient service. Walk to Huandao cycling road. A solid mid-range choice for first-time Xiamen visitors.

hotels.name: Orange Hotel Xiamen Zhongshan Road
hotels.rating: 8.2/10 Booking.com | best budget near Zhongshan pedestrian street
hotels.tip: Best budget option in central Xiamen — right on Zhongshan Road pedestrian street, walk to Gulangyu ferry in 15 minutes. Clean, modern rooms. Praised for helpful staff. Orange Hotel brand known for consistent cleanliness. Very popular with budget Chinese and SEA travellers.

huangshan.name: Huangshan (Yellow Mountain)
huangshan.recommended_base_reason: Tangkou town at the mountain base is most convenient — shuttle buses to all entrances, many hotels and guesthouses
huangshan.seasonal_note: Winter (Nov–Feb) has best snow scenery and rime ice, fewer crowds. Spring (Mar–May) has blooming rhododendrons. Autumn (Sep–Nov) has stunning foliage. Summer is peak crowds.

clusters.huangshan-front: Front mountain — Welcoming Pine, Lotus Peak, West Sea Grand Canyon, Bright Summit (Guangming Ding)
clusters.huangshan-back: Back mountain — Shixin Peak, Dreaming Brush (Mengbi Shenghua), Monkey Gazing at Sea
clusters.tangkou-base: Mountain base town — accommodation, shuttle buses, restaurants
clusters.hongcun-xidi: Two UNESCO Huizhou ancient villages, 30 min from base
clusters.tunxi-oldstreet: Huangshan city old street — tea, antiques, Hui architecture, city base

attractions.name: Huangshan Sunrise (Bright Summit)
attractions.seasonal_note: Best cloud sea conditions: after light rain, with high humidity (>80%) and light wind. Winter mornings most dramatic. Summer 7–8am fine but less cloud.
attractions.tip: Huangshan's most coveted experience — watching dawn light break over a sea of clouds between mountain peaks. Guangming Ding (1,860m) is the best panoramic sunrise point. Stay overnight at Beihai Hotel (book months ahead for peak season) to walk to the viewpoint at 5am. Clear sky is good but a sea of clouds is transcendent — check mountain weather app night before. Arrive at viewpoint 1–1.5hrs before sunrise to get a spot. Dress warmly — summit is 10–15°C colder than base.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Welcoming Guest Pine (Yingke Song)
attractions.tip: China's most famous individual tree — a 1,300-year-old Huangshan pine growing from a granite cliff at a 45° angle, 'welcoming' visitors. The image that defines Huangshan. Near Yuping Pavilion on the front mountain cable car upper station — very easy to reach. Get there early to photograph without crowds. The surrounding area has dozens of equally remarkable named pines: Black Tiger Pine, Dragon Claw Pine, Twin Pines.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: West Sea Grand Canyon
attractions.tip: The most dramatic hiking route on Huangshan — a network of cliff-hugging walkways descending into a cloud-filled canyon between sheer granite walls. The 'Valley Floor Cable Car' (¥80 single) saves 2hrs of steep climbing back up. The Paiyun Pavilion viewpoint over the west sea of clouds is the best wide-angle view in Huangshan. Allow 4–5hrs to do justice. Not suitable for young children or elderly — steep and physically demanding. Morning fog in the canyon is magical.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Hongcun Ancient Village
attractions.seasonal_note: Dawn mist at Moon Pond is best Mar–May and Sep–Oct. Autumn maple colours at nearby Tachuang are spectacular.
attractions.tip: Called 'China's most beautiful painting' — 500-year-old Hui village designed in the shape of a buffalo. The crescent-shaped Moon Pond (Yuezhaotang) reflecting whitewashed walls in morning mist is one of China's most iconic images. Arrive BEFORE 7am for empty lanes (ticket office opens 7am). The承志堂 (Chengzhi Hall) private mansion has extraordinary wood carvings — don't skip. Film location for Crouching Tiger Hidden Dragon. 3-day ticket validity. Combine with Xidi on same day (20 min drive).
attractions.description: A 500-year-old Hui minority village designed in the shape of a buffalo — the crescent-shaped Moon Pond reflecting whitewashed Huizhou walls in morning mist is one of China's most iconic images. Arrive before 7am for empty lanes and the extraordinary light that prompted UNESCO designation.

attractions.name: Xidi Ancient Village
attractions.tip: UNESCO twin village to Hongcun — nicknamed 'Huizhou Architecture Museum'. Better preserved Ming-Qing architecture, 300+ ancient buildings. The Hu Wenguan Memorial Archway at the entrance is stunning. Less commercialised than Hongcun — more genuine village life. Maze-like lanes are a joy to wander. The local tofu (Huizhou-style braised tofu) sold from street stalls is excellent. Combine with Hongcun — 20 min apart.
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Huizhou village — 300 well-preserved Ming and Qing dynasty residences in a village that has changed little in 500 years. The carved wood screens and ink wash painting-worthy streetscapes make this the most photographically rewarding village in eastern China.

attractions.name: Tunxi Old Street (Huangshan City)
attractions.tip: 1.5km of authentic Song-dynasty style street lined with antique shops, tea merchants, brush-and-ink sellers and Hui snack stalls. The real stuff — Maojian tea, Huizhou inksticks, old woodblock prints. The Liyue Old Street section has live music in the evenings. Huangshan airport/train station is in Tunxi — make this your first or last stop.
attractions.description: The best-preserved Huizhou commercial street in China — 1.5km of Song dynasty shophouses selling Huangshan specialties including Mao Feng tea, She ink sticks and Huizhou inkstone. The morning light on the whitewashed walls is extraordinary and the street empties completely by 8am on weekdays.

attractions.name: Shixin Peak & Back Mountain Loop
attractions.tip: The back mountain approach via Yungu Cable Car gives access to Shixin Peak — where the famous 'Black Tiger Pine' and 'Connected Love Pines' grow. Less crowded than the front mountain route. The circular loop from cable car upper station through Shixin Peak to North Sea viewpoints takes 4hrs of hiking. Photographers often prefer back mountain for less-visited viewpoints. Take Yungu cable car up, Yuping cable car down for the classic front-back traverse.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Huangshan (Yellow Mountain) that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Stinky Mandarin Fish (Chou Guiyu)
food.tip: Huangshan's most famous dish — fish preserved for 7 days until slightly fermented, then braised in spicy Hui sauce. The smell is pungent (like strong cheese) but the taste is extraordinary — rich, umami, melt-in-mouth. Must try at least once. Best at Hongcun's 得月楼 (Deyue Restaurant) or Tangkou base restaurants.

food.name: Huangshan Barbecue Sesame Cakes
food.tip: Flaky, sesame-coated pastry filled with pork and preserved vegetable. Baked in a tandoor-style oven on the street. Hot from the oven is magical — ¥3 each. Perfect hiking snack. Buy extras — they keep well. Available everywhere in the region.

food.name: Mao Tofu (Huizhou Fermented Tofu)
food.tip: Tofu covered in white mould (like brie), pan-fried with chilli sauce. A Huizhou speciality sold from village street stalls — ¥2–3 per piece. Looks alarming, tastes amazing. The fermentation gives it a creamy, rich flavour. Most interesting food experience in the region.

food.name: Mountain Top Provisions
food.tip: Food on the mountain summit costs 3-5x what it costs at the base. Buy energy bars, instant noodles, water and snacks at Tangkou before going up. The mountain hotels serve basic meals (¥60–120/dish) — fine for dinner if staying overnight but overpriced for day trippers.

food.name: Tangkou Base Town Restaurants
food.must_order: Stinky Mandarin Fish (臭鳜鱼), Huangshan sesame cakes
food.tip: Tangkou has many good Huizhou restaurants at reasonable prices. The stinky mandarin fish is the must-try signature dish — fermented for 7 days then braised in spicy Hui sauce. Intimidating smell, extraordinary taste. The sesame sesame cakes (¥3–5 each) from street stalls are the best hiking snack. Stock up before going up the mountain.

food.name: Hongcun Moon Pond Cafes
food.near: Inside Hongcun village, next to Moon Pond
food.must_order: Coffee reflected in Moon Pond, Huizhou osmanthus cake
food.tip: Several cafes around the Moon Pond have second-floor terraces looking down at the reflected Hui village architecture. The early morning (6:30–8am) Moon Pond reflection with mist is one of China's iconic landscape photos. Sit upstairs with coffee and wait for the light to be perfect. Photography-focused visitors come specifically for this view.

food.name: Xidi Village Courtyard Restaurants
food.must_order: Mao tofu (毛豆腐), Huizhou braised tofu, wild mountain vegetables
food.tip: Family-run restaurants inside Xidi village serve genuine Huizhou home cooking. Mao tofu (fermented tofu covered in white mould, pan-fried with chilli) is sold from street stalls for ¥2–3 each. The braised tofu with dried mushrooms is a Huizhou staple. Cheaper and more authentic than Hongcun's tourist restaurants.

food.name: Tunxi Old Street Tea Merchants
food.must_order: Maojian green tea, Keemun black tea
food.tip: The tea merchants on Tunxi Old Street sell Huangshan's legendary teas direct: Huangshan Maojian (yellow mountain fine tip) and Keemun black tea (the world's most famous black tea, used in Earl Grey). Buy 50–100g for tasting at the source — a fraction of city prices. Shop owners brew samples — take your time and taste multiple grades.

food.name: Summit Mountain Instant Noodles
food.near: Any mountain hut on Huangshan summit
food.must_order: Instant noodles, boiled eggs, summit tea
food.tip: At 1,800m in sub-zero temperatures, a ¥25 bowl of instant noodles from a mountain hut is one of China's most satisfying food experiences. The mountain hut restaurants serve basic food at 3–5x base prices but nothing has ever tasted better than hot noodles after 4 hours of mountain hiking. Bring your own snacks but budget for one summit meal.

hotels.name: Beihai Hotel (Summit)
hotels.tip: THE summit hotel for sunrise. 10-min walk to Guangming Ding viewpoint. Book 3+ months ahead for peak season — impossible to get last-minute. Basic rooms but the location is everything. Worth the premium for the sunrise experience.

hotels.name: Shilin Hotel (Summit)
hotels.tip: Alternative summit option to Beihai — west side of mountain, near Paiyun Pavilion. Good for west sea cloud views. Slightly cheaper than Beihai.

hotels.name: Tangkou Boutique Inns
hotels.tip: Base town guesthouses — ¥150–300/night. Good starting point if doing day hike only. 10-min walk to shuttle buses. Many include free shuttle to the cable car. The town has good restaurants and supermarkets for supplies.

hotels.name: Hongcun Moon Pond Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Stay inside Hongcun village itself — waking up before tourists arrive to photograph the empty Moon Pond is priceless. Old mansions converted to boutique guesthouses. Book 1+ month ahead. Rooms often have courtyard access.

hotels.name: Huangshan Y悦榕庄 (Banyan Tree Huangshan)
hotels.tip: Luxury Banyan Tree property near Hongcun — stunning Huizhou-inspired villas with mountain views, spa and infinity pool. The rooftop sundowner watching the mountains fade at dusk is extraordinary. Best luxury base in the region.

hotels.name: InterContinental Huangshan Resort
hotels.rating: 9.1/10 Trip.com | best luxury at the mountain base
hotels.tip: IHG luxury at Huangshan's base — shuttle to cable car station included, excellent spa to recover after mountain hiking, beautiful pool. Praised for breakfast quality and English-speaking staff. The most comfortable base for exploring the mountain and villages.

hotels.name: Huangshan Yupinglou Hotel (Summit)
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com (for summit experience value) | only accessible by cable car
hotels.tip: 'Luxury' here means paying a premium to sleep ON the mountain summit — right next to Welcoming Guest Pine and perfect sunrise positioning. Rooms are basic 3-star but the location is everything. Book 3 months ahead in peak season. This is where sunrise photography happens.

hotels.name: Manxin Hotel Hongcun
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Trip.com | best design hotel in Hongcun
hotels.tip: The finest designed hotel inside Hongcun village — Hui-style architecture with modern boutique interiors, courtyard and mountain views. Wake up before tourists and photograph empty Moon Pond. Praised for exceptional service and atmosphere. One of Anhui's best boutique hotels.

hotels.name: Tunxi Old Street Guesthouses
hotels.rating: 8.2+/10 average for good options
hotels.tip: Multiple boutique guesthouses in restored Hui buildings right on Tunxi Old Street — ¥200-400/night. Wake up on the most authentic historic street in the region. Walk to tea shops and antique markets immediately. Pick ones with recent 8.0+ ratings on Trip.com.

hotels.name: Tangkou Town Guesthouses
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 average for good options
hotels.tip: Town guesthouses at the mountain base — ¥150-280/night. Many include free shuttle to cable car. Communal atmosphere, meet other hikers. Pick ones with recent 8.0+ Trip.com reviews. Perfect for budget travellers doing a day hike and not staying on the summit.

nanjing.name: Nanjing
nanjing.recommended_base_reason: Daxinggong/New Street area (大行宫/新街口) is central — 15 min to all major sights, excellent metro connections, restaurants and accommodation density
nanjing.seasonal_note: Spring (Mar–Apr) for cherry blossoms at Jiming Temple and Xuanwu Lake. Autumn (Oct–Nov) for Qixia Mountain maple leaves. Summer is very hot and humid (Nanjing is China's 'furnace city').
highlights: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum stone animal road

clusters.zhongshan-scenic: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum, Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum, Meilinggong (Meiling Palace), Music Terrace — all in Zhongshan National Park
clusters.qinhuai-district: Confucius Temple, Qinhuai River, Old Menbdong Street, Zhanyuan Garden, Jiangnan Gongyuan
clusters.daxinggong: Presidential Palace, Nanjing Museum, 1912 commercial district, Jiming Temple, Xuanwu Lake
clusters.city-north: Xuanwu Lake Park, Nanjing Massacre Memorial, Yunzhen Museum

attractions.name: Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum (Zhongshan Ling)
attractions.tip: The mausoleum of Sun Yat-sen (founder of modern China) — 392 steps leading to the blue-roofed memorial hall. Arrive before 8am to walk the 'Jade Tunnel' (Cuigu) boulevard in peaceful morning light without crowds. Free but MUST book via official WeChat mini-program. The blue ceramic tile roof that changes colour with cloud and light is extraordinary. Combine with Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum (15 min walk) on same morning. Not suitable for elderly with knee problems — 392 steps are steep.
attractions.description: The mausoleum of modern China's founding father — 392 steep steps lead through forested hills to a blue-roofed memorial hall where Sun Yat-sen lies in state. The view looking back down from the summit over the pine-covered hills toward Nanjing is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum & Stone Animals Road
attractions.seasonal_note: The 'Best 600 Metres' — Stone Animal Road is lined with ancient ginkgo and maple trees that turn gold in October. Enter before 6:30am for free access.
attractions.tip: The 'Sacred Way' — 615m lined with pairs of lions, camels, elephants, unicorns and humans in perfect symmetry, dating from 1398. Enter before 6:30am for FREE (also avoids crowds). The stone animals are extraordinary — among the most impressive Ming-era sculptures in China. Kids love 'spotting all the animals'. The mausoleum itself (¥70 for full access) is impressive but the stone road is the highlight. Combine with Zhongshan Ling in same morning visit.
attractions.description: The mausoleum of the Hongwu Emperor who founded the Ming dynasty — the Sacred Way lined with stone animals and officials leading to the mausoleum is one of China's most serene historical walks, through ancient forest that has been sacred for 600 years.
attractions.photo_query: ming xiaoling mausoleum nanjing sacred way

attractions.name: Confucius Temple & Qinhuai River Night Cruise
attractions.chinese: 夫子庙 & 秦淮河夜游
attractions.tip: GO AT NIGHT — daytime is just a shopping street. After 7:30pm the entire riverbank lights up — lanterns reflected in the water, decorated wooden boats (画舫) drifting past, illuminated bridges. Take the West Line river cruise (¥40) past Peach Leaf Crossing and Wu Yi Lane while a 评弹 (Suzhou opera) singer performs on the boat. Kids love the lanterns and boats. Avoid the tourist restaurants inside — eat at Old Menbdong (老门东) 1.3km away, then walk back to the river. 39th Lantern Festival (Jan–Feb) here is extraordinary.
attractions.description: The ancient examination complex where China's imperial scholars were tested sits beside a canal whose illuminated banks at night, lined with traditional wooden boats and red lanterns, create the most atmospheric evening scene in the Yangtze Delta.
attractions.photo_query: qinhuai river nanjing confucius night lantern

attractions.name: Presidential Palace
attractions.tip: China's largest surviving Republican-era (民国) architectural complex — where Chiang Kai-shek governed and where the Communist takeover of Nanjing happened in 1949. Beautiful Chinese garden alongside Western-influenced official buildings. Walking through the Sun Yat-sen office, Chiang Kai-shek's residence and the conference halls feels like a living history lesson. The adjacent 1912 Street (1912街区) has good cafes. Note: closed Mondays.
attractions.description: The most historically layered building in China — from Qing dynasty garden to Taiping Heavenly Kingdom palace to Republic of China presidential residence, this single complex witnessed every major turning point in modern Chinese history. The 1912 wing where Sun Yat-sen was inaugurated is extraordinary.

attractions.name: Nanjing Museum
attractions.tip: China's third largest museum (after Palace Museum and National Museum). Free but MUST book 7 days ahead — tickets are extremely hard to get. Don't miss: the Republican Era Gallery where you can dress in 1930s Shanghai clothing for photos (enormously popular with women visitors), the Jiangnan Ancient Civilization exhibition spanning 8,000 years, and the Digital Gallery. Closed Mondays. The Da Bao En Temple replica gateway from the National Treasures TV show is here.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Nanjing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Nanjing Massacre Memorial Hall
attractions.chinese: 侵华日军南京大屠杀遇难同胞纪念馆
attractions.tip: Solemn, important and deeply moving — memorial to the 300,000 killed in the 1937 Japanese occupation. Mandatory experience for understanding modern China-Japan relations and 20th century history. The architectural design (bare stone, silence-inducing spaces) is extraordinary. Not appropriate for children under 13. Book far in advance — one of the most difficult tickets to get in China. Allow 2 hours minimum. The Eternal Flame courtyard and bone pit display area are the most affecting sections.
attractions.description: A must-visit museum in Nanjing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Xuanwu Lake Park
attractions.seasonal_note: Cherry blossom season (late March–early April) makes this Nanjing's most magical spot
attractions.tip: Large lake park inside the city walls — paddle boats, walking paths, island parklets. Best during cherry blossom season when the lakeside turns pink. The Meiling Palace (美龄宫) gardens at the edge of the park are excellent in spring. Evening summer activities include night boating. Jiming Temple across the road has rooftop views over the lake and Ming city wall.
attractions.description: A must-visit nature in Nanjing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Red Mountain Forest Zoo
attractions.tip: Went viral on Chinese social media in 2024 for the animals' hilariously expressive personalities — particularly the polar bear and otters. China's most popular zoo for the animals' 'internet famous' behaviours. The gorilla who stands upright like a human is a particular star. Genuine animal welfare focus — spacious enclosures. Book online ahead — gets very busy on weekends. Kids and parents equally entertained.
attractions.description: A must-visit zoo in Nanjing that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Salted Duck (Yán Shuǐ Yā)
food.tip: Nanjing's most famous food — locals say Nanjing people eat 100 million ducks a year. Lightly salted and steamed duck, served cold, white skin with a jade-like gleam. Subtle, clean flavour — nothing like Beijing roast duck. Buy from a market deli counter rather than a restaurant. Jianing Road Duck Market (¥60–80 per duck) is the authentic way to buy.

food.name: Duck Blood Vermicelli Soup
food.tip: Nanjing breakfast staple — light broth with glass noodles, duck blood cubes, duck offal and tofu. Sounds extreme, tastes comforting. ¥10–15 from street stalls near Confucius Temple area early morning. The local breakfast experience.

food.name: Old Menbdong Food Street
food.tip: Restored Qing dynasty neighbourhood near Confucius Temple — much less touristy than the temple area, better food, more authentic architecture. 蒋有记锅贴 (Jiang Youji pan-fried dumplings) and 蓝老大糖藕粥 (lotus root porridge) are famous. Great for dinner before a Qinhuai river night cruise.

food.name: Old Menbdong (老门东) Food Street
food.near: Old Menbdong district, adjacent to Confucius Temple
food.must_order: Pan-fried dumplings (锅贴), lotus root porridge, duck blood soup
food.tip: The best food strip in Nanjing — restored Qing dynasty neighbourhood with better food and more authentic atmosphere than the Confucius Temple tourist area. Jiang Youji (蒋有记锅贴) pan-fried dumplings are legendary — crispy-bottomed, soup-filled. Lotus root porridge (藕粉) and sugar-lotus root (糖桂花藕) are Nanjing specialities. Walk here before the Qinhuai River night cruise.

food.name: Salted Duck Market (盐水鸭市场)
food.near: Jianing Road Duck Market, Gulou District
food.must_order: Half a salted duck — ask them to chop it
food.tip: Nanjing's most famous food — lightly salted whole duck, served cold, with delicate white skin and clean flavour. Buy from a deli counter in the market at ¥60–80 per duck (or half). Eat at the market or take back to hotel. The definitive Nanjing eating experience. Every local has a preferred duck vendor — ask hotel staff.

food.name: 1912 Street Cafes & Bars
food.must_order: Creative cocktails in Republican-era building
food.tip: A cluster of bars and cafes in restored Republican-era (民国) buildings — the architectural style unique to Nanjing. Best at night when the buildings are lit up. Walk here after visiting the Presidential Palace next door. The contrast of 1930s architecture with modern cafe menus is very Nanjing.

food.name: Zhongshan Scenic Area Teahouse
food.near: Zhongshan National Park, near Ming Xiaoling Mausoleum
food.must_order: Purple mountain tea, chrysanthemum cake
food.tip: Small teahouses within Zhongshan National Park offer a peaceful rest after climbing the 392 steps of the Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum. The ginkgo alleys in October turn golden and the park becomes one of Nanjing's most beautiful spaces. Rest with tea while other tourists hurry past.

food.name: Duck Blood Vermicelli Soup Breakfast
food.near: Near Confucius Temple area, early morning
food.must_order: Duck blood vermicelli soup (鸭血粉丝汤)
food.tip: The quintessential Nanjing breakfast — glass noodles, duck blood cubes, duck liver and tofu in a light fragrant broth. Available from ¥10–15 at any local breakfast stall near the Confucius Temple area before 9am. The texture of fresh duck blood is silky and mild. If you have the courage to try it, it's genuinely delicious.

hotels.name: InterContinental Nanjing
hotels.tip: Best central location — walking distance to Presidential Palace and Nanjing Museum. City views from upper floors.

hotels.name: Nanjing Grand Hotel
hotels.tip: Historic landmark hotel — the revolving restaurant on the top floor (Nanjing's first skyscraper) still draws locals for Sunday brunch. Central and reliable.

hotels.name: Qinhuai River Boutique Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Staying by the Qinhuai River means you can walk to the night cruise and Confucius Temple in 2 minutes. Boutique guesthouses in renovated Qing buildings. Best for the evening atmosphere.

hotels.name: Budget Hotels near New Street
hotels.tip: New Street (新街口) area has dozens of chain hotels at ¥200–400/night — Holiday Inn Express, Ibis, Home Inn. Central metro access to all sights. Best value area.

hotels.name: W Nanjing
hotels.rating: 9.2/10 Trip.com | best design hotel in Nanjing
hotels.tip: Marriott's W brand in Nanjing CBD — dramatic contemporary design, excellent rooftop bar with city views. Walk to Presidential Palace and Nanjing Museum. Very popular with younger international travellers. Outstanding breakfast.

hotels.name: Mandarin Oriental Nanjing
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com | Forbes Five-Star
hotels.tip: Forbes Five-Star in the heart of Nanjing shopping district — Xinjiekou metro at the door, walk to major museums and Presidential Palace. The Mandarin's legendary service standards. Excellent Chinese restaurant.

hotels.name: Hilton Nanjing Riverside
hotels.rating: 8.6/10 Booking.com | Yangtze River views, good value
hotels.tip: IHG mid-range with Yangtze River views — walk to Nanjing Eye pedestrian bridge and waterfront. Good breakfast with local Nanjing dishes. Praised for helpful staff. Good option for families wanting riverside location without luxury prices.

hotels.name: Ibis Nanjing Confucius Temple
hotels.chinese: 南京夫子庙宜必思酒店
hotels.rating: 8.3/10 Booking.com | unbeatable Qinhuai location
hotels.tip: Best budget option in Nanjing — right at the Confucius Temple and Qinhuai River. Walk to the night cruise in 2 minutes. Accor reliability. Free breakfast. Staff praised for friendliness. Perfect base for evening Qinhuai River exploration.

hotels.name: CHAO Hotel Nanjing
hotels.rating: 8.7/10 Booking.com | praised for design and rooftop
hotels.tip: Trendy design hotel popular with young professional travellers — rooftop bar with city views, co-working spaces, curated local art throughout. Walk to 1912 Street and Presidential Palace. More personality than chain hotels. Good mid-range choice for design-conscious travellers.

hotels.name: Zhongshan Scenic Area Guesthouses
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 average
hotels.tip: Boutique guesthouses near Zhongshan Scenic Area — wake up and walk to Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum and Ming Xiaoling before crowds arrive. ¥250-400/night. Much quieter than city centre hotels. Pick verified 8.0+ options on Trip.com.

qingdao.name: Qingdao
qingdao.recommended_base_reason: Shinan district puts you at the heart of all old-city sights — Zhanqiao Pier, Eight Great Passes, Signal Hill, all walkable
qingdao.seasonal_note: May–Jun: mild and beautiful. Jul–Aug: beer festival and beach crowds — hot, packed. Sep–Oct: ideal. Nov–Apr: cold but peaceful, best for photography.

clusters.zhongshan-road: Zhanqiao Pier, Catholic Cathedral, Middle Mountain Road, Qingdao Station, Old City core
clusters.eight-passes: Eight Great Passes villas, Second Beach, Princess House (Gongzhu Lou), Flower House (Huashi Lou)
clusters.wusi-square: Fifth Fourth Square, Olympic Sailing Centre, Lover's Dam, Little Wheat Island
clusters.signal-hill: Signal Hill Park, University Road red-wall photo spot, Old Town artsy district
clusters.laoshan: Laoshan mountain scenic area — full standalone day

attractions.name: Zhanqiao Pier (栈桥)
attractions.seasonal_note: Nov–Apr is seagull season — hundreds of seagulls can be fed from the pier. Summer is very crowded.
attractions.tip: Qingdao's most iconic symbol — 440m pier stretching into the Yellow Sea, ending with the classical Chinese 回澜阁 (Huilan Pavilion). Free to walk. In seagull season (November to April) the pier is surrounded by hundreds of seagulls — extraordinary photographs. At low tide, walk the reef rocks beside the pier for a different angle. Arrive before 8am for empty pier photos — by 9am it's packed. The rocky beach beside the pier at low tide is excellent for children to explore.
attractions.description: Qingdao's most iconic structure — a 440-metre stone pier extending into the Yellow Sea, ending in the Chinese-style Huilan Pavilion. In winter the pier is surrounded by hundreds of seagulls that can be fed by hand, and the sea spray freezes on the railings creating ice sculptures.
attractions.photo_query: zhanqiao pier qingdao sea winter seagull

attractions.name: Eight Great Passes (Bā Dà Guān)
attractions.seasonal_note: Spring: cherry blossom along韶关路 (Shaoguan Road). Autumn: golden ginkgo leaves. Each road has a different tree species planted.
attractions.tip: Called 'World Architecture Museum' — a residential neighbourhood where 200+ villas in 20 different national styles (German, Russian, British, French, Danish, Swiss, Greek, Spanish) sit under tree-lined avenues. Each of the 8 roads is named after a famous pass (gate) and planted with a different tree species. The 公主楼 (Princess House, Danish style, ¥20) and 花石楼 (Flower Stone House, Gothic style, ¥8) are the must-enter buildings. Walking the shaded lanes is free. Spring cherry blossoms on Shaoguan Road are legendary.
attractions.description: The world's most eclectic residential neighbourhood — 200 villas built in 20 different national architectural styles from 1903-1940, now standing under avenues where each street is planted with a different tree species. Cherry blossoms in spring and ginkgo in autumn are extraordinary.
attractions.photo_query: eight great passes qingdao villa european

attractions.name: Signal Hill Park
attractions.tip: Best viewpoint in Qingdao old town — the rotating observation platform (¥10 extra) does a full 360° giving the 'red roof green trees blue sea' panorama Qingdao is famous for. Best at 4–5pm when afternoon light turns rooftiles golden. The former German Governor's residence (Tsingtau Governors Palace) is visible directly below. Kids enjoy the rotating platform. From the park, walk down to University Road artsy district — red-brick walls with street art, boutique cafes.
attractions.description: The best free viewpoint in Qingdao — a hilltop park with panoramic views of the red-roofed German colonial city below, the sea beyond and Zhanqiao Pier extending into the bay. The mushroom-shaped Meteorological Observation Station at the summit is Qingdao's most charming architectural detail.
attractions.photo_query: signal hill park qingdao viewpoint red roof

attractions.name: Tsingtao Beer Museum
attractions.tip: Inside the original 1903 Tsingtao brewery — the real thing. Tour includes: German brewery equipment, 120 years of history, and most importantly, two glasses of fresh draft beer from the original tanks (included in ticket). The unfiltered 原浆 (yuanjiang) fresh beer available here is not sold anywhere else — extraordinarily fresh and complex. Book ahead especially weekends. Collect the iron stamp from every exhibit for your visitor card. The museum gift shop has limited-edition bottles not sold elsewhere.
attractions.description: The world's most enjoyable beer museum — inside the original 1903 German brewery that created Tsingtao beer, you taste fresh unfiltered beer straight from the source at the end of the tour. The German industrial heritage and the story of how a colonial beverage became China's most exported product is fascinating.
attractions.photo_query: tsingtao beer museum qingdao german brewery

attractions.name: Laoshan Mountain
attractions.tip: 'Sea-on-mountain' — dramatic Taoist mountain where granite peaks meet the Yellow Sea. The Yangkou Route is the classic: panoramic sea views from rocky summits, ancient Taoist monasteries, Laoshan spring water (used to brew Tsingtao beer). Juyong Valley (仰口) section has a sea-view beach at the foot of the mountain. Scenic bus loops the full area. Tea gardens produce Laoshan green tea — buy direct at the farms, 3x cheaper than city shops. A standalone full day from Qingdao.
attractions.description: China's most famous Taoist mountain — granite peaks meeting the Yellow Sea, 4th-century cliff inscriptions, ancient monastery tea gardens producing Laoshan green tea used in Tsingtao beer, and dramatic sea views from 1,133-metre summits.

attractions.name: Olympic Sailing Centre & Lover's Dam
attractions.tip: 2008 Olympics sailing venue — now a waterfront park. The 'Lover's Dam' (情人坝) jetty has excellent city skyline and harbour views. Best at 7:30pm when the light show begins — the 'May Wind' sculpture at Fifth Fourth Square lights up, and you can watch sailboats returning to port. Sail boat trips available (¥150/hr). Good sunset photography point. Combine with Little Wheat Island (小麦岛) 5 min walk — dramatic rock formations, very Instagrammable.
attractions.description: A must-visit attraction in Qingdao that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Catholic Cathedral of St Michael
attractions.tip: China's only officially consecrated Catholic cathedral — stunning Gothic twin spires visible across old Qingdao. Built 1934 by German missionaries. Interior with coloured glass windows is beautiful. ¥10 to enter inside. Exterior photos free. Adjacent square has an artsy café cluster. Combine with nearby Zhongshan Road and Signal Hill in the same morning.
attractions.description: A must-visit culture in Qingdao that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

attractions.name: Qingdao Polar Ocean World
attractions.chinese: 青岛海昌极地海洋公园
attractions.tip: Excellent aquarium and polar animal park — polar bear shows, beluga whales, sea lions, penguins, plus a full aquarium with underwater tunnel. Kids under 1.2m free. The beluga whale training session and polar bear feeding are the highlights. Convenient afternoon activity after morning old town sightseeing. Book online for discount.
attractions.description: A must-visit aquarium in Qingdao that showcases the best of local culture and history. Ask your hotel concierge for the best time to visit and any advance booking requirements.

food.name: Spicy Stir-Fried Clams (Chǎo Gélí)
food.tip: Qingdao's unofficial city dish — small clams wok-fried with chilli, garlic and spring onion in a dry, fragrant style. Order a big bowl with cold draft beer in a plastic bag (袋装啤酒, ¥5/bag). This combination — clams + cheap plastic-bag beer + outdoor table — is the authentic Qingdao experience. Eat at Jiulong Restaurant or any busy seafood stall near Taidong market.

food.name: Freshly Tapped Laoshan Spring Beer
food.tip: The unforgettable Qingdao experience: buying freshly tapped unfiltered beer from a street vendor who fills your plastic bag from a tap. ¥5 for a litre bag, cold and foamy. The original Tsingtao Brewery on Dengzhou Road (Dēngzhōu Lù) is the best source. The Taidong night market also sells bag beer — pair with clams and grilled seafood at outdoor tables.

food.name: Fresh Seafood Market (Tuan Dao Market)
food.tip: Qingdao's best local seafood market — arrive 7–9am for the freshest catch. Razor clams, sea urchin, swimming crabs, sea cucumbers, mantis shrimp. Buy live seafood and have it cooked at a nearby restaurant (加工费). Much cheaper than restaurants and higher quality than tourist seafood streets. Always confirm price per jin before agreeing.

food.name: Tsingtao Beer Fresh Tap Stalls
food.near: Dengzhou Road (登州路) Brewery Street, near Tsingtao Brewery
food.must_order: Fresh unfiltered Tsingtao (原浆) in plastic bag — ¥5/litre
food.tip: The most authentic Qingdao experience — buying freshly tapped unfiltered beer in a plastic bag from a street vendor near the original brewery. ¥5 for 1 litre, ice cold and foamy. Eat alongside with hot clams from a nearby stall. This combination — plastic bag beer + clams + outdoor table — is Qingdao's defining food culture.

food.name: Spicy Clam Restaurants (辣炒蛤蜊)
food.near: Near Tuan Dao Market or local seafood areas, Shinan
food.must_order: Spicy stir-fried clams, beer sausage, shrimp
food.tip: Qingdao's unofficial city dish — small clams wok-fried with dry chilli and garlic. Order a bowl (¥30–50) with cold draft beer. The combination is extraordinary. Jiulong Restaurant or any busy seafood restaurant near Taidong market area is reliable. Always confirm price before ordering — tourist areas charge double.

food.name: Eight Great Passes Villa Cafes
food.near: Eight Great Passes villa district, Shinan
food.must_order: Coffee on villa terrace, German cake
food.tip: The cafe scene inside the Eight Great Passes is excellent — independently run cafes in or adjacent to the 1930s colonial villas. Spring brings cherry blossom on Shaoguan Road (韶关路) — the pink canopy over the cafe tables is extraordinary. Autumn ginkgo trees turn golden. Visit slow, walk between cafes.

food.name: Second Beach Seafood Restaurants
food.chinese: 第二海水浴场海鲜餐厅
food.near: Second Beach (第二海水浴场), near Eight Great Passes
food.must_order: Fresh sea urchin, grilled scallops, sea cucumber
food.tip: The restaurant strip adjacent to Second Beach is where Qingdao locals eat — better quality and lower prices than tourist seafood areas. Always confirm price per jin (500g) before agreeing to anything. Grilled scallops with garlic and glass noodles (¥35/6 pieces), fresh sea urchin in shell (¥15–20 each) are outstanding.

food.name: Qingdao Taidong Night Market
food.must_order: Korean BBQ, local seafood skewers, fresh fruit desserts
food.tip: Qingdao's best night market — Korean and Chinese food stalls, fresh seafood grilled over charcoal, cold plastic-bag beer everywhere. The Taidong pedestrian street at night is young, loud and vibrant — very different from the historic old city. Best 8–11pm. Much more local than the tourist Zhongshan Road area.

hotels.name: Westin Qingdao
hotels.tip: Best luxury option — sea views from upper floors, next to Fifth Fourth Square. Walking distance to sailing centre and beaches.

hotels.name: Eight Great Passes Sea-View Guesthouses
hotels.tip: Stay INSIDE the Eight Great Passes area — waking up in a 1930s villa neighbourhood with Second Beach 200m away. Book 'Qihai' guesthouse (栖海) on booking.com — 180° sea views, right on the coastal boardwalk. Worth spending a bit more for this location.

hotels.name: Hilton Qingdao Golden Beach
hotels.tip: On the golden sand beach in Huangdao district — great if beach is the priority. 40 min from old city but best beach access. Worth it for families who want beach over sightseeing.

hotels.name: Li'Yuan Historic Inn (Old City)
hotels.tip: Classic Qingdao 'Li Yuan' courtyard guesthouse — a type unique to Qingdao, blend of German and Chinese architecture. ¥150–250/night. Authentic old-city atmosphere. Walking distance to Zhanqiao Pier and Cathedral.

hotels.name: Intercontinental Qingdao
hotels.rating: 9.0/10 Trip.com | praised for location and service
hotels.tip: Best luxury near the Olympic Sailing Centre — commanding sea views, excellent dining, walk to Lover's Dam and Fifth Fourth Square. Praised consistently for service quality and English-speaking staff. Close to the best Qingdao nighttime waterfront.

hotels.name: Le Méridien Qingdao
hotels.rating: 8.8/10 Booking.com | praised for modern design and views
hotels.tip: Marriott's Le Méridien in a prime seafront location — modern design hotel with excellent views of Jiaozhou Bay. Very good breakfast, praised for attentive service. Walk to Olympic Sailing Centre and major waterfront attractions. Family rooms available.

hotels.name: Sofitel Qingdao
hotels.chinese: 青岛索菲特银海大饭店
hotels.rating: 8.6/10 Booking.com | praised for Eight Great Passes proximity
hotels.tip: Accor 5-star in the Eight Great Passes villa district — walking distance to the colonial villas and Second Beach. Good value 5-star. Praised for breakfast and helpfulness. Walk to the beach in 5 minutes. One of the best-located hotels for sightseeing in Qingdao.

hotels.name: Zhuhai Hotel Qingdao (Li Yuan)
hotels.rating: 8.5/10 Booking.com | authentic Qingdao li yuan courtyard
hotels.tip: A renovated 'li yuan' — Qingdao's unique courtyard housing blend of German and Chinese architecture. Only a handful of rooms around a central courtyard. Walk to Zhanqiao Pier and Cathedral. The most authentic Qingdao accommodation experience. Book well ahead.

hotels.name: Ibis Qingdao Central
hotels.chinese: 青岛市中心宜必思酒店
hotels.rating: 8.1/10 Booking.com | best-located budget in Qingdao
hotels.tip: Best budget in central Qingdao — near Qingdao Railway Station, walk to Zhanqiao Pier in 10 minutes. Accor reliability, clean rooms, free breakfast. Metro access to all areas. Praised for location and clean facilities. Essential to book ahead in summer peak.

hotels.name: Huangdao Golden Beach Resort Hotels
hotels.rating: 8.0+/10 average for rated options
hotels.tip: Multiple resort hotels on Qingdao's best beach (Golden Beach, Huangdao) at budget prices — 40 min from old city but beach access is exceptional. ¥200-400/night. Good for beach-focused travellers on a budget. Pick Trip.com 8.0+ options near the beachfront.
